gmw_00000002.name: NGC 7582
gmw_00000002.overview: NGC 7582 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1309.5166, arXiv:1608.06513] belonging to the interacting Grus Quartet [arXiv:1303.3511]. It hosts a highly obscured, variable Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0302442, arXiv:2203.04585] surrounded by a circumnuclear starburst ring [arXiv:1908.03552, arXiv:2302.11897].

sources.summary: The paper models the central stellar population of NGC 7582, describing it as a starburst/AGN galaxy dominated by young stars, a recent burst, clumps, and a Wolf-Rayet feature.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is defined as a Starburst/AGN galaxy, since its optical and X-Ray spectra reveal both characteristics. In this work, we show the results of a stellar population modeling in a datacube taken with the Gemini South telescope. We found that 90 of the light in the field of view is emitted by stars that are less than 1 billion years old.

NGC 7582 has a typical H II region spectrum in the optical but its X-Ray emission is typical of a Seyfert 1 galaxy . In this work our goal is to analyze the stellar population located in the 3.5'' x 5'' central region of NGC 7582.

A strong event occurred 6 million years ago. This young stellar population shows nearly solar metallicity and is concentrated in three clumps that might be associated with the ionizing sources of the three observed H II regions. One of the clumps, located nearly 1.4'' West and 1.0'' North of the AGN presents a Wolf-Rayet feature, shown in Figure fig2 .

sources.summary: NGC 7582 is described as a Grus Quartet Seyfert with optical type-2 classification, near-IR broad-line evidence, nuclear star-forming regions, high-ionization lines, and detected CO.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 NGC 7582 is a Seyfert galaxy at a distance of 20.6 Mpc. It is a member of the interacting Grus Quartet deVacouleurs1975 . The nuclear activity is classified as Type 2 because no broad lines are detected in the optical Heisler1997 . However, Reunanen2003 reported a broad component in the near-IR Br line. The hard X-ray luminosity of the AGN is 2.0 10 ^ 41 erg s ^ 1 Piconcelli2007 .

The nuclear region of NGC 7582 harbors young star-forming regions that dominate the ionization of the nuclear gas Riffel2009 . In the mid-IR IRS spectrum of NGC 7582 the [ Ne 5 ] and [ O 4 ] high-ionization emission lines are detected Tommasin2010 . The CO lines from J_ up =4 to 12 are detected in the \ data.

sources.summary: The paper reports high-SNR H I observations of NGC 7582, deriving its inclination and systemic velocity, finding asymmetric rotating-disk kinematics consistent with a warp, central absorption, and nearby companion-related diffuse emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is an SB(s)ab galaxy RC3 at a distance of 20.6 2.4 Mpc. Our observation of this galaxy also has high SNR. The inclination derived from the kinematics is 67.9 , compared with 68.2 from HyperLeda Paturel03 . The systemic velocity is 1588 km s ^ 1 as fit by H 1 . Its velocity field shows the clear signature of rotation and is asymmetric (Fig. fig:ngc7582 ), suggesting a warped disk.

Finally, there are two companions of NGC 7582 observed in the same velocity range and sitting 9 and 12 to the north-east respectively. These are the companion galaxies NGC 7590 and NGC 7599. There is faint diffuse emission 13 long that extends from NGC 7590 to the west of NGC 7582 with a closest distance to NGC 7582 of 5 5 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7582 as dust-lane affected, with irregular stellar continuum maps, a central stellar-light deficit likely caused by foreground dust, and measurable kinematics despite low central CO EW.

quotes.quote: The two exceptions are NGC 7172 and NGC 7582, for both of which the maps exhibit extreme irregularities coinciding with known dust lanes Bianchi2007,Smajic2012 . These pass across their nuclei and are clearly visible in optical HST F606W images Malkan1998 . It is difficult to quantify the Ks-band extinction and correct it with present data.

NGC 7582 is an example of the latter case, where the foreground dust lane across the circumnuclear region causes the stellar light asymmetry. However we cannot rule out that its stellar light deficit is intrinsic. The central stellar light deficit in NGC 4593 is likely to be intrinsic, and is unambiguously observed in both SINFONI and NICMOS radial flux profiles. Furthermore, we try to measure the bulge S e rsic profile solely based on HST/NICMOS/F160W image for these two galaxies by using GALFIT algorithm. NGC 7582 has strong asymmetric photometry, so that we cannot constrain the bulge S e rsic profile properly.

Notably, NGC 7582 has a small CO EW in the centre but the velocity and velocity dispersion can still be measured reliably even close to the AGN. The reason is that it is the brightest galaxy in our sample in the K-band, and the noise in the data is small enough that we can extend the CO EW dilution limit to 2 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7582 has the most complex spectrum in the sample, requiring partial line-of-sight absorption and a Compton-thick, high-covering torus.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 NGC 7582 exhibits the most complex X-ray spectrum of the AGN discussed here. Its \ is known to be variable and multiple layers of absorption have been invoked in previous spectral analyses rivers+2015-ngc7582 . We find it necessary to include a non-zero parameter _2 (as defined in sec:model-borus02 ) in order to account for partial absorption along the line of sight; without it, the residuals show a significant excess below 4.5 keV. For the first \ observation considered here, under different assumptions for , we always find \ consistent with (3.6 0.4) 10^ 23 \ and _2=0.10 0.04 (i.e.

The ^2 curve shown in the lowest pair of panels in fig:sweep shows a very well defined minimum at 24.5 . At this , both \ and \ are narrowly constrained to 0.90 0.03 . Additionally fitting for the torus column density yields =24.0 0.1 , and does not affect the other model parameters. NGC 7582 therefore seems to have a CT torus that covers 90 of the sky as seen from the SMBH, yet we observe it through a hole with roughly an order of magnitude lower column density.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GMOS-IFU data of NGC 7582, finding optical AGN signatures, broad hydrogen features, scattered AGN light, and arguing that it is a Seyfert 1 galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 was identified as a Starburst galaxy in the optical but its X-Ray emission is typical of a Seyfert 1 galaxy . We analyzed a datacube of this object obtained with the GMOS-IFU on the Gemini-South telescope. After a subtraction of the stellar component using the starlight code , we looked for optical signatures of the AGN.

We detected a broad H component (figure fig1 ) in the source where identified the AGN in an HST optical image. We also found a broad H feature (figure fig2 ), but its emission reveals a extended source. We suggest that it is the light of the AGN scattered in the ionization cone. We propose that NGC 7582 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 7582 as a Seyfert nucleus with bright 610 MHz emission, bar-aligned radio structure, and Faraday-depth evidence for real polarisation.

quotes.quote: The brightest emission emanates from the nuclei of NGC 7552 and NGC 7582. Extended emission is detected at 610 MHz along the bar of NGC 7552. There is clear radio structure along the bar of NGC 7582 through to the outer northern and southern edges of the galaxy. These knots of emission are also visible in H 1999AJ...118..730H .
The fractional polarisations are 0.87 0.02 and 0.70 0.01 for NGC 7552 and NGC 7582 respectively.

Following RM Synthesis of the brightest polarised pixel in NGC 7582, the peak in Faraday space occurs at a FD of 1.83 0.22 rad m ^ 2 - a plot of the Faraday spectrum is shown in Fig. GalaxyA-Faraday_spectrum . FDs in the immediately surrounding bright pixels are all negative, varying between 9 rad m ^ 2 to 1 rad m ^ 2 . The consistently negative, and non-zero, FDs are again indicative of the detection not resulting from instrumental polarisation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7582 as a highly inclined barred spiral with an obscured AGN, star-forming disk, dust lane, extended MIR emission, strong PAH/[Ne II] features, and mostly star-formation-related MIR emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is a highly inclined barred spiral galaxy. This galaxy has an obscured AGN surrounded by a star-forming disk and a dust lane crossing over the nucleus. The subarcsecond resolution Q-band image reveals a bright cluster towards the south, at 200 pc from the nucleus, which is not present in the N-band image. There is also faint emission extending out to 630 pc from the southeast to the northwest (PA 155 deg).

The Spitzer/IRS spectrum shows important 6.2 and 11.3 m PAH features and also strong [ Ne II ] emission. On the contrary, the spectrum shows really weak AGN indicators such as [ S IV ] and [ Ne V ] . Therefore, the bulk of this MIR emission is likely related with SF activity.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7582 as a nearby changing-look Seyfert 2 AGN, using archival X-ray observations to characterize variability and a clumpy absorber changing between Compton-thin and Compton-thick states.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 ( z = 0.005264 ; D=22.5 Mpc) is a highly variable, changing-look AGN. In this work, we explore the X-ray properties of this source using \ and \ archival observations in the 3-40 keV range, from 2001 to 2016. NGC 7582 exhibits a long-term variability between observations but also a short-term variability in two observations that has not been studied before.

In this work, we study NGC 7582, a nearby ( z=0.005264 , D=22.5 Mpc) Seyfert 2 galaxy with a changing-look behaviour. estimated the black hole mass to be M_ BH = 5.5^ +3.2 _ 2.3 10^7 M _ by modelling the gas dynamics in the mid-infrared. This source has been observed in X-rays in several occasions since the late 70's. associated the X-ray source 2A 2315-428, detected with the Ariel 5 survey, with the galaxy NGC 7582.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7582 as an obscured NLS1-like Seyfert 2, noting its [O III] cone, unusual broad-line optical state, rapid X-ray variability, low 2001 flux, and steep primary power law.

quotes.quote: In this Letter , we present X-ray evidence for three type 2 counterparts (NGC 7582, NGC 5506 and NGC 7314) of NLS1s. NGC 7582 is a classical Seyfert 2, with well defined [ O III ] cone (Sosa-Brito et al. 2001; Aretxaga et al. 1999; Storchi-Bergmann Bonatto 1991). Aretxaga et al. (1999) observed NGC 7582 in an unusual optical state during July 1998 when it showed broad components of H , H ( FWHM 12000 ) blue-shifted by 2500 . The flux state was relatively high during the optical event, and then decreased gradually to its typical type 2 state in October 1998.

NGC 7582 showed a gradual decline in its X-ray intensity from a count rate of 0.3 to 0.2 counts s^ 1 in addition to small amplitude rapid variability. The most extreme variability occurred when the count rate decreased by 0.19 0.056 counts s^ 1 from an elapsed time of 5700 50 to 5900 50 .
In addition to the extreme variability, the three AGNs show steep 2-12 primary power-law (NGC 7582: = 2.26_ 0.17 ^ +0.14 ; NGC 5506: = 1.98_ 0.02 ^ +0.05 ; NGC 7314: = 2.19_ 0.06 ^ +0.09 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7582 as a Seyfert 2 spiral with extended X-ray emission, complex AGN and diffuse emission, and X-rays shaped by star-formation processes rather than group gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582, z=0.0054 The 5^ extent of the X-ray emission of NGC 7582 is shown in Fig. f:ol . Two other galaxies, NGC 7590 and NGC 7599, (not shown in Fig. f:ol ) also reveal extended X-ray emission on the scales of 1-2^ , each one centered on a galaxy. Our study confirms the lack of diffuse X-ray emission from this group reported by Mulchaey Zabludoff (1998) based on the ROSAT data. NGC 7582 is a well studied Seyfert 2 galaxy. Its hard (2-10 keV) X-ray emission has been detected in many X-ray surveys (Ward et al. 1978) and the X-ray spectrum of this AGN is complex (.f. Turner et al. 2000). Detailed spectroscopic analysis shows a presence of emission from the AGN and complex diffuse emission from the bright spiral galaxy (NGC 7582). Since the origin of the emission from the spiral galaxy is likely to be a mixture of emission from hot gas, point sources and supernova remnants, which is quite different from the rest of the sample, we omit this system from further analysis.

In Koribalski (1996) the HI image of the NGC 7582 group was presented. A comparison shows that the X-ray emission is embedded within the H_ , and that the shape of the X-ray emission is determined by the star-formation processes, as opposed to the case where the hot gas fills the potential of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7582 as a classic Seyfert 2 with changing broad lines, extended north-south mid-infrared emission, weak 12.9 micron sources, and a slightly resolved 11.9 micron peak.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 NGC 7582 is a classic Seyfert 2 galaxy which, during a period of five months in 1999 showed broad lines characteristic of a Seyfert 1 galaxy, which may indicate the presence of a patchy torus. . 50 of the flux found in extended emission with a peak offset from the center of the extended emission. The extended emission is seen elongated along the South-North direction.

The 12.9 image shows two weak sources to the North and to the South of the main peak, also clearly seen in the 12.9 image of . The 11.9 image shows one slightly resolved peak surrounded by extended emission. Flux at 11.9 is very similar to that of , although the 11.9 image they present shows considerably less extended emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7582 as an inclined Sab Seyfert 2 with a star-forming disk, asymmetric ionization cone, classical mixing sequence, and radiation-pressure-dominated ENLR.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is also a Sab galaxy with a clear star-forming disk CidFernandes01 . The Seyfert 2 nucleus powers a bright extended ionization cone Morris85 . The galaxy is significantly inclined (i = 68.2 ^ ) and our WiFeS observations cover the central 2.6 3.9 kpc ^2 region at a resolution of 130 pc.

We also observe mixing between star formation and AGN activity across the nuclear region of NGC 7582, although the largest line ratios are offset from the nucleus and are associated with an asymmetric ionization cone which is clearly visible in the 3-color image. The large dispersion in the mixing sequence of NGC 7582 is likely to be attributable to metallicity and/or ionization parameter variations within this ionization cone (see Section subsec:model_results ).
The line ratio distributions of IC 1816 and NGC 7582 are therefore prototypical examples of what we term `classical mixing sequences'.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 7582 in high-resolution X-rays, finding complex changing-look AGN absorption and emission from sub-pc scales to a galactic absorber.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 ( z=0.005254 ) is an X-ray bright ( F_ 2-10 keV 3-8 10^ 12 ; Bianchi09,Piconcelli2007 ; hereafter B09 and P07, respectively) Seyfert 2 galaxy, which has been observed in the X-ray band several times: from the early days of X-ray astronomy with Einstein and Ginga to the most recent X-ray observatories , \ and . \ is classed as one of the prototype ``changing look" AGN as it has shown remarkable variability on different time-scales both in the optical ( Aretxaga1999,Turner2000 ) and in the X-ray band. Evidence for a variable and complex X-ray absorber has been reported since the low spectral resolution observations performed with ASCA and \ ( Xue1998,Turner2000 ).

Continuum modeling The first inspection of the \ data shows a spectrum dominated by emission lines with an extremely low continuum, suggesting that NGC 7582 was in a Compton thick state for the duration of the observation. This is in agreement with the analysis of the XMM-RGS data presented by P07, where, despite the larger aperture of the RGS data with respect to the \ gratings, it was already found that the soft X-ray emission in NGC 7582 has a strong contribution from a photoionised gas. Although only few ``changing look" AGN and Seyfert 2 galaxies have been observed long enough with both high spatial and spectral resolution it is now clear that ``Changing look'' AGN like NGC 1365 and NGC 7582 have a favorable line of sight such as we can view all the stratification of these absorbers/emitters.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7582 as a nearby Seyfert II with an [O III] outflow extending to about 3 kpc, a dust-obscured receding cone, and a literature-consistent mass outflow rate.

quotes.quote: In order to show the application of \ to real sources, we selected a sample of three nearby Seyfert-II galaxies, from the MAGNUM survey, namely NGC 4945, Circinus and NGC 7582. In the case of Circinus and NGC 7582 we used the [ OIII ] 5007 emission line to map outflow properties; for NGC 4945 instead, we used [ NII ] 6584 , since [ OIII ] emission is highly obscured by dust in this edge-on galaxy (see e.g. The projected distance covered by the outflow ranges from 1 kpc in Circinus, to 3 kpc in NGC 7582 Juneau2022 .

The receding cone in NGC 7582 and NGC 4945 is more dust obscured than the approaching one, as usually observed in Seyfert galaxies and AGN in general. We found mass outflow rates of 1.9 10^ 3 M_ yr ^ 1 , 0.2 10^ 2 M_ yr ^ 1 and 1.4 10^ 2 M_ yr ^ 1 for NGC 4945, Circinus and NGC 7582, respectively. The M _ out obtained for NGC 7582 is consistent with the high-ionization counter-part of 0.7 10^ 2 M_ yr ^ 1 , inferred by Davies2020a using VLT/Xshooter spectra and covering the inner 300 pc.

sources.summary: NGC 7582 is described as a Sy2 galaxy with a nuclear starburst and outflow; its clumpy-torus fit fails at 18.2 microns and gives unreliable parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582. NGC 7582 is a Sy2 galaxy that harbors a nuclear starburst surrounding the active nucleus Cid01,Sosa01,Wold06,Bianchi07 and presents a nuclear outflow Morris85 . The fit with the clumpy models shown in Figure sy2_1 does not reproduce the 18.2 point, and the resulting probability distributions of the parameters are very different compared with the rest of Sy2 galaxies discussed here (except for NGC 1808). Low values of are more probable ( < 29 ), as are extremely small numbers of clouds ( N_0 < 2). High values of q ( > 2.5 ) and low values of the _V ( < 27 ) are preferred.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7582 as starburst-contaminated but with nuclear silicate absorption; its strong near-IR emission and absorption make CLUMPY fitting unsuccessful.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 shows some PAH residuals at the position of the 7.7 m PAH feature after the template subtraction, and therefore, again it is not simple to asses the presence of the residual 10 m feature. However, results from small aperture, ground-based spectroscopy of NGC 7582 shows that in this case the presence of the silicate absorption is related to the nuclear source (AH11).

The most extreme silicate absorption features are seen in NGC 7172 and NGC 7582, while NGC 1194, IRAS 04385-0828, NGC 4388 and NGC 5506 correspond to more moderate cases. Using S_ 10 to quantity the depth of the silicate feature ( S_ 10 = F_ /F_ , , where F_ corresponds to the flux at the deepest point of the absorption feature and F_ , corresponds to the flux in the interpolated continuum) we find that NGC 7172 and NGC 7582 have S_ 10 2.0 and 1.4 , respectively, while the other 4 sources listed above have S_ 10 values between 0.7 and 1 . We find that it is not possible to properly fit NGC 5506, NGC 7172 and NGC 7582 with the set of current CLUMPY models because a combination of deep silicate absorption and SEDs with strong near-IR emission is not available.

sources.summary: NGC 7582 has a star-forming disk, extended high-excitation ionization cone and counter-cone, apparent outflow, embedded H II regions, and prior HST and long-slit data.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582: This galaxy displays a star-forming disk CidFernandes01 out of which a high excitation, bright, and extended ( > 15 arcsec, 1.3kpc) ionisation cone with an opening angle of 110 emerges . The counter-cone also appears (although heavily reddened) on the far side of the disk, and extends across the field. The ionisation cones appear to be in outflow -see also Morris85 . Many fine \ regions are embedded in the star forming disk. An HST red continuum image is available Malkan98 , , and long slit spectroscopy has been obtained by Fraquelli03 .

sources.summary: The paper finds bright polar mid-IR emission in NGC 7582, perpendicular to the ALMA torus, plus circumnuclear star-formation dust and molecular outflow evidence.

quotes.quote: The PSF-subtracted images of six out of the twelve galaxies present bright extended mid-IR emission (Fig. fig:midIRimages ). These are NGC 1365, NGC 3227, NGC 5506, NGC 5643, NGC 6300, and NGC 7582. The contours show that the extended emission is detected up to at least a 20 _ bk level. For NGC 4388 we also detected extended mid-IR emission at a lower significance but in a similar orientation as that derived by using a different dataset. The sizes of the extended mid-IR emission range from approximately 50 pc in NGC 6300 to 190 pc in NGC 5506.

In NGC 1365, NGC 3227, and NGC 7582 as well as the inner regions of NGC 5506, the extended mid-IR emission appears to be approximately perpendicular (in projection) to the extended ALMA 870 m continuum emission. The latter for these four galaxies is identified as the torus. In NGC 5643, the orientation of the extended mid-IR emission is mostly along PA_ MIR = 60-70 ^ , which is nearly perpendicular (in projection) to the nearly north-south ALMA 870 m continuum emission. Finally, in NGC 6300 the extended mid- and far-IR emission show similar orientations, and thus the extended mid-IR emission is along the equatorial direction of the torus.

NGC 3227, NGC 4388 and NGC 7582 as well as NGC 1068 show evidence of molecular outflows on physical scales larger than the torus up to a few hundred parsecs, see and GB21 Davies2014, GarciaBurillo2019, DominguezFernandez2020 , which are more likely due to the interaction between the AGN wind and/or a radio jet if present and molecular gas in the disk of the host galaxy. NGC 5643 and NGC 5506 appear to have extended both polar and equatorial mid-IR emission. In NGC 6300 the mid-IR emission might be due to dust in the equatorial direction of the torus as well as in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7582 as a nearby spiral AGN with circumnuclear star formation, an AGN outflow, and obscuration that protects naphthalene-bearing PAHs.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is a nearby spiral galaxy, located at a distance of 23.0 Mpc and a luminosity of log L _X = 42.53 0.38 erg s ^ 1 Jensen2017 . NGC 7582 presents a circumnuclear region of active star formation and an AGN-powered outflow, making it an ideal site for studies focusing on the feeding and feedback processes in which AGN lead to an increase or decrease in star formation Riffel2009 .

In the case of NGC 7582, the half-life times obtained show that naphthalene can present comparable times, or even higher, than that necessary for the injection of PAHs in the interstellar medium (2.5 10^ 9 yr, Jones1994 ). The conditions of NGC 7582 are optimal for the formation of these molecular species and this is due to the fact that, although its x-ray luminosity of 2.11 10^ 54 eV s ^ 1 is considerably large, its high N_H value of 4.90 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 makes it the most obscured AGN source amongst those investigated herein. Therefore, we can speculate that the thick dust surrounding the circumnuclear region is an effective blocker, which exerts a high degree of protection for PAHs, including smaller PAHs such as naphthalene.

sources.summary: The paper maps nuclear and surrounding mm-wave components in NGC 7582, matching them to MIR structures that may trace dusty young clusters.

quotes.quote: 97: NGC 7582 Our mm-wave image reveals nuclear emission ( a ) and also five surrounding components labeled b , , d , e , and f . Hints on the origins can be obtained by comparing the ALMA image with a MIR [ Ne II ] emission line image of . The MIR image revealed six areas with bright MIR emission, labeled M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, and M6. The mm-wave components of b , , d , e , and f appear to coincide with M1, M2, M3+M4, M5, and M6, respectively. The authors described that the six MIR structures may be due to young star clusters embedded in dust and, otherwise, to clusters that are older and obscured by a screen of dust.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7582 as a dusty central starburst where CaT dilution is marginal and much weaker than dilution in optical absorption lines.

quotes.quote: In NGC 7582 there is a marginal hint of dilution. From optical work we know that this galaxy hosts a very dusty central Starburst, so it could represent a case where the Starburst light does contribute to the flux in the CaT range with a non negligible fraction. However, even in this most favorable case the nuclear CaT is diluted by less than 15 with respect to its value at 500 pc. In contrast, the optical absorption lines are much more diluted: At the wavelengths of the CaK, G-band and MgIb lines, the dilution factors are 70, 60 and 57 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper maps the inner region of Seyfert galaxy NGC 7582, finding hot circumnuclear dust, a young star-forming ring, and stellar and ionized-gas kinematic structures.

quotes.quote: We analyse two-dimensional near-IR K- band spectra from the inner 660 315 pc ^2 of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 7582 obtained with the Gemini GNIRS IFU at a spatial resolution of 50 pc and spectral resolving power R 5900. The nucleus harbors an unresolved source well reproduced by a blackbody of temperature T 1050 K, which we attribute to emission by circumnuclear dust located closer than 25 pc from the nucleus, with total mass of 3 10^ 3 M _ . Surrounding the nucleus, we observe a ring of active star formation, apparently in the galactic plane, with radius of 190 pc, an age of 5 Myr and total mass of ionized gas of 3 10^6 M _ . The radiation of the young stars in the ring accounts for at least 80 of the ionization observed in the Br emitting gas, the remaining being due to radiation emitted by the active nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7582 as a deep-silicate-feature Seyfert with imperfect photometry/spectroscopy fits, X-ray absorbers, dust lanes, and compatible ionization-cone limits.

quotes.quote: As for the pure Seyfert 2 NGC 7172, the two galaxies with relatively deep silicate features in absorption (NGC 5506 and NGC 7582), it is possible to do a simultaneous fit of the photometry and mid-IR spectroscopy, although it is not a good fit. Imanishi (2000) measured an unusual ratio between the depths of the 3.4 m carbonaceous dust absorption and the 9.7 m silicate dust in NGC 5506. He suggested that the obscuration towards the nucleus of NGC 5506 could be ascribed mostly to dust in this host galaxy ( > 100-pc scale) and not to the obscuring torus. This may also reflect differences in grain populations (see Roche et al. 2007). The X-ray emission of NGC 7582 indicates the presence of at least two absorbers of columns densities of 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 and 4-5 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , with the latter probably associated with large scale obscuration (Bianchi et al. 2009).

For three AGN with relatively deep 9.7 m silicate features and hosted in inclined galaxies with prominent dust lanes (NGC 5506, NGC 7172, and NGC 7582), we were not able to fit simultaneously the photometric SEDs and mid-IR spectroscopy.
For the two galaxies with _ cone 100 (NGC 5506 and NGC 7582, Table 1) the fitted _ torus are compatible with the approximate limits set by the observations ( _ obs 40-50 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7582's nuclear spectrum, identifies it as one of three objects overlapping VISIR data with consistent features, and reports a hint of PAH emission.

quotes.quote: MIR spectra Fig. fig:spectrum shows the nuclear spectrum of NGC 7582 (open dots and gray area).

Our sample has three objects in common with the sample reported by Honig10a using VISIR/VLT data (NGC 5643, IC 5063, and NGC 7582). The shape and detected features in the three VISIR spectra in common with our sample are consistent with those reported with T-ReCS.
A hint of the PAH feature is also present in other 4 AGN (NGC 1386, NGC 5643, NGC 7172, and NGC 7582).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7582 as a well-known Seyfert-2 comparison object, describing its XMM-Newton spectrum as similar to V4641 Sgr and fit by complex absorption plus distant reflection.

quotes.quote: To facilitate a comparison with V4641 Sgr, we downloaded the 2007 XMM-Newton observation of the well-known Seyfert-2 NGC 7582 (observation 0405380701; see Bianchi et al. \ 2009).

Figure fig:combined shows the Chandra spectrum of observation 4451 plotted with the XMM-Newton spectrum of the Seyfert-2 NGC 7582. The similarities between the two spectra are clear. Typical of Seyfert-2 AGN, Bianchi et al. \ (2009) find that the spectrum of NGC 7582 is fitted acceptably by a combination of strong and complex absorption, and distant reflection.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 7582 as a highly inclined barred spiral with an obscured nucleus, composite AGN-starburst nuclear spectrum, circumnuclear star-forming disk, and starburst-dominated MIR emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is a highly inclined barred spiral galaxy with an obscured nucleus. The nuclear spectrum has been studied as a composition between AGN and starburst Veron97 . The AGN is surrounded by a powerful SF disc (major axis diameter 400 pc) and a dust lane crossing over the nucleus Morris85, Riffel09 . Asmus14 concluded that the starburst dominates the total MIR emission.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 7582's visible kiloparsec circumnuclear starburst ring, treats its nucleus as an exception to the sample's resolved Gaussian widths, and flags PSF-subtraction ambiguity.

quotes.quote: All nuclei were clearly detected, whereas for NGC 5135 and NGC 7582 also the known kiloparsec-scale circum-nuclear starburst rings are partly visible (see, e.g., asmus_subarcsecond_2014 for discussion).
All nuclei, except those of NGC 5135 and NGC 7582, show Gaussian-fit major axis full width half maximum (FWHM) values that are 10 to 20 larger than those of the PSF references (median: 12 ).

Nevertheless, we obtain similar extended structures after PSF subtraction in all cases but NGC 7582. In the latter case, the PSF of observatory standard is clearly more elongated than our own and thus its subtraction leads to significant negative residuals hiding any real structure.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 7582 as a Grus Quartet starburst spiral with two tidal H I tails stripped from its outer disk and linked kinematically to the galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 . --- Koribalski (1996) find extended H i tails associated with the starburst spiral galaxy NGC 7582, which is a member of the Grus Quartet. The two tails, probably stripped from NGC 7582's outer disk by tidal interactions, span 18 ' (105 kpc). Their kinematical signature suggests a connection to the southern (approaching) side of NGC 7582. Together the two tidal tails have an H i mass of nearly 2 10^9 M _ , which is approximately 15 of the total H i mass detected in the Grus Quartet.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7582 as an example where near-infrared imaging at intermediate inclination shows both a bar and a shorter peanut feature.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is a particularly interesting galaxy since, despite its intermediate inclination (approximatively 65^ ), near infrared imaging (Quillen 1997) allows us to see both the bar and the peanut. One can now see that the peanut has a considerably shorter extent than the bar. Similar comments can be made for NGC 4442 (Bettoni Galletta 1999). As already explained above, this is not an argument against the fact that peanuts are just edge-on bars (Kormendy Kennicutt 2004), but a confirmation of it, since it is predicted from orbital structure theory and from simulations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7582 as a hard-spectrum AGN requiring a dual-absorber model, using Beppo-SAX results to derive absorption and intrinsic luminosity corrections.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 A simple absorbed power-law fit to the ASCA data gave = 1.2 and N_ H =6 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . The best description of its X-ray spectrum was given by Turner2000 from the Beppo-SAX data. Their model included an intrinsic power-law component of = 1.95 absorbed by two components: a totally covering N_ H,22 = 14.4 absorber and a thicker absorber with N_ H,22 = 160 blocking 60 of the X-ray source. We employ this model for de-absorption (see above) and the sum of these two absorbing columns for the representative neutral column density.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 7582, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 This source varied between the 2000 Chandra observations and later XMM-Newton observations, as well as between the 2001 and 2005 XMM-Newton observations; the Chandra and XMM-Newton observations were fit independently. The Chandra spectra were moderately affected by pileup ( 30 - 49 according to the jdpileup model in Sherpa) and were therefore fit with a pileup model component in XSpec, with only , the grade migration parameter, allowed to vary; the pileup component was discarded before calculating the flux and the Fe K EW. The broad-band Chandra spectra were best fit by a double absorbed power law whereas the XMM-Newton spectra required a thermal component and Gaussian components at the Fe K energy and at ionized Fe K energies ( 6.72 keV for the 2005 observation and 6.97 keV for the 2001 observation) However, in the local (3 - 8 keV) fits, this higher energy Gaussian is more consistent with Fe K emission, with a best-fit centroid energy of 7.08 keV for the 2001 observation, and shifts to a best-fit centroid energy of 6.91 keV for the 2005 observation.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 7582's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is a highly inclined barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 23 4.6 Mpc springob_erratum:_2009 with an obscured AGN surrounded by a powerful star formation disc (major axis diameter 4 0.4 kpc) and a dust lane crossing over the nucleus morris_velocity_1985,regan_using_1999,riffel_agn-starburst_2009 . The AGN belongs to nine-month BAT AGN sample and has complex X-ray source with varying obscuration (e.g. piconcelli_xmm-newton_2007,bianchi_how_2009 ).

sources.summary: The appendix classifies NGC 7582 as a non-detection, noting Tombesi10 velocity inconsistency and suggesting higher-energy telescopes may improve future constraints.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582 is classed as a non-detection due to similar issues as above. One can also observe that the 0.255c result obtained by Tombesi10 is not consistent with the variability spectra, as there is no peak at 9 keV. However, future investigation with telescopes of higher energy ranges could provide more accurate data at these high energies where the EPIC-pn detector reliability dwindles.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7582 as a nearby AGN host whose galactic substructure and newly reported kinematically distinct core are tied to obscuration, collimated outflows, and AGN-driven pressure.

quotes.quote: We present a case study of nearby galaxy NGC 7582, for which we find evidence that galactic substructure plays an important role in affecting the collimation of ionized outflows as well as contributing to the heavy active galactic nucleus (AGN) obscuration.
We report for the first time a kinematically distinct core (KDC) in NGC 7582, on a scale of 600 pc.

The interpretation of a classical mixing sequence favors AGN radiation as the dominant source of static pressure in the ENLRs of NGC 7582 rather than gas pressure; dop02,gro04a , supporting a scenario where the AGN can therefore drive galactic-scale outflows.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC7582 as a CIR-SMBH outlier whose central ring, active star formation, high velocity dispersion, and intense nuclear starburst may affect its CIR value.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, NGC4594 and NGC7582, show significant deviation from this correlation. NGC7582 is reported to host a ring with active star formation within the pc scale radius ( 190  pc) surrounding the nucleus of the galaxy, along with a high stellar velocity dispersion 2009MNRAS.393..783R . The intense nuclear starburst activity 2001RMxAC..11..133C,2007MNRAS.374..697B can affect its CIR value.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7582 as a barred group galaxy with variable medium-energy emission, diffuse soft emission perpendicular to its disk, and a fainter resolved companion.

quotes.quote: The soft emission of NGC 7582 (Figure fig:phsn7582 a) is extremely diffuse and extends mostly perpendicular to the plane of the galaxy, as expected from a superwind.
NGC 7582 and its companion are also separated by about 2 and resolved in HRI Sch98 and PSPC images (Figure fig:n7582x4 ).
NGC 7582 NGC 7582 is a group member and contains a bar.

sources.summary: NGC 7582 is described as structurally extended and variable between observations; compared with heavily absorbed composites, its lower column lets the AGN shine through.

quotes.quote: In the examples where the X-ray emission is extremely extended or exhibits a great deal of structure, such as NGC 1068, Mrk 266, and NGC 7582, this assumption is incorrect, and the resulting imaging and spectral fits disagree somewhat.
The \ and \ observations of each of these galaxies, NGC 6221 and NGC 7582, were separated by a year or two.
When N_H is small, we see the AGN shining through (e.g., NGC 7582), while for the heavily absorbed cases (e.g., NGC 5135) the galaxies dominate the spectra.

sources.summary: The paper reports compatible BeppoSAX results for NGC 7582, fitting excess high-energy emission with strong cold reflection while noting an alternative double-absorber interpretation.

quotes.quote: NGC 7582: Turner et al. (2000) present a detailed analysis of the same BeppoSAX data. The results are compatible, but these authors prefer a different interpretation for the high energy emission in excess of the simple powerlaw, which we fit with a strong cold reflection component. Arguing that the required reflection component is too high, they suggest that the intrinsic continuum (which is completely covered by gas with N _ H 1.4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , in agreement with our results), is also absorbed by a second screen with N _ H 1.6 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , and a covering factor of 60 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7582 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy that temporarily showed Seyfert 1 behavior, with torus obscuration, variable X-ray absorption, and nuclear stars supporting mixed interpretations.

quotes.quote: The case of NGC 7582, a classical Sy 2 that suddenly mutated into a Sy 1 (Aretxaga et al. 1999a), is probably one of the most compelling examples where the SN explanation works, although this is by no means a unique solution. Many of the nuclear properties of NGC 7582 support a unified scheme where the true Sy 1 nature is hidden by an obscuring torus: a sharp-edged [ O III ] outflow in the form of a cone is observed (Morris et al. 1985); optical spectropolarimetry does not reveal a hidden broad-line region, but since the far-IR colours 60 m 25 m are very red, the absence has been taken as support for an edge-on thick torus able to block even the light scattered towards the observer (Heisler et al. 1997); indeed a large column density of neutral H ( N_H 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) also blocks the hard X-rays (Warwick et al. 1993), but this absorption is variable and decreased ( N_H 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) at the time of the transition between Seyfert types (Turner et al. 2000). The presence of stars in the nucleus is also firmly established: Morris et al. (1985) found a steep gradient of H perpendicular to the [ O III ] cone, which they interpret as a 1 kpc disk of H II regions, and Oliva et al. (1995) find strong photospheric CO 1.62, 2.29 m absorptions.

gmw_00000003.name: NGC 1266
gmw_00000003.overview: NGC 1266 is a nearby lenticular field galaxy [arXiv:1104.2326, arXiv:1211.3438] that hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1211.3438, arXiv:2211.01641] and exhibits a post-starburst stellar spectrum [arXiv:1708.02683, arXiv:2106.05982]. The active nucleus drives a massive, multiphase molecular outflow [arXiv:1410.4556, arXiv:1803.02357] that is shock-excited [arXiv:1311.3993, arXiv:1409.5916] and suppresses star formation [arXiv:1410.4556, arXiv:1803.02357].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 1266 as a nearby non-interacting S0 with a compact molecular nucleus and massive molecular outflow, arguing that star formation is insufficient and the wind is likely AGN driven.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a powerful molecular wind from the nucleus of the non-interacting nearby S0 field galaxy NGC 1266. The single-dish CO profile exhibits emission to 400 \ and requires a nested Gaussian fit to be properly described. Interferometric observations reveal a massive, centrally--concentrated molecular component with a mass of \ \ and a molecular outflow with a molecular mass of \ . The molecular gas close to the systemic velocity consists of a rotating, compact nucleus with a mass of about \ \ within a radius of \ pc. This compact molecular nucleus has a surface density of \ \ pc ^ 2 , more than two orders of magnitude larger than that of giant molecular clouds in the disk of the Milky Way, and it appears to sit on the Kennicutt-Schmidt relation despite its extreme kinematics and energetic activity.

We interpret this nucleus as a disk that confines the outflowing wind. A mass outflow rate of \ \ leads to a depletion timescale of \ Myr. The star formation in NGC 1266 is insufficient to drive the outflow, and thus it is likely driven by the active galactic nucleus (AGN).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1266 as a nearby early-type field galaxy with a compact molecular disk, AGN-driven molecular outflow, and VLBA evidence for a compact low-level AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266 is a nearby field galaxy observed as part of the \ survey (Cappellari et al. 2011). NGC 1266 has been shown to host a compact ( < 200 pc) molecular disk and a mass-loaded molecular outflow driven by the AGN (Alatalo et al. 2011). Very Long Basline Array (VLBA) observations at 1.65 GHz revealed a compact (diameter < 1.2 pc), high brightness temperature continuum source most consistent with a low-level AGN origin. The VLBA continuum source is positioned at the center of the molecular disk and may be responsible for the expulsion of molecular gas in NGC 1266. Thus, the candidate AGN-driven molecular outflow in NGC 1266 supports the picture in which AGNs do play a significant role in the quenching of star formation and ultimately the evolution of the red sequence of galaxies.

NGC 1266 is a nearby ( D 29.9 Mpc) field early-type galaxy that CO observations have shown to harbor a compact molecular disk ( _ gas > 10^4 M_ pc ^ 2 ) and a mass-loaded ( M_ gas > 10^9 M_ ; M > 13 M_ yr ^ 1 ) molecular outflow (Alatalo et al. 2011). Alatalo et al. \ (2011) showed that the molecular gas could not be driven by SF alone and concluded that an AGN was the most likely culprit. Very Large Array (VLA) observations revealed a compact radio core coincident with the weak detection of hard X-rays with Chandra (Alatalo et al. 2011). Subsequent VLBA observations confirmed the presence of a compact, high brightness temperature ( T_b > 10^7 K) radio core (Fig. fig1 ), further supporting existing evidence that NGC 1266 hosts a low-level AGN.

sources.summary: NGC 1266 is presented as a nearby lenticular with an AGN-powered molecular outflow, inefficient residual molecular gas, compact far-infrared emission, and star formation suppressed by AGN-driven turbulent injection.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266 is a nearby lenticular galaxy that harbors a massive outflow of molecular gas powered by the mechanical energy of an active galactic nucleus (AGN).

Here we present detailed observations of the gas and dust continuum of NGC 1266 at millimeter wavelengths. Our observations show that molecular gas is being driven out of the nuclear region at M _ out 110 M_ yr ^ 1 , of which the vast majority cannot escape the nucleus. Only 2 M_ yr ^ 1 is actually capable of escaping the galaxy. Most of the molecular gas that remains is very inefficient at forming stars.

The far-infrared emission is dominated by an ultra-compact ( 50 pc) source that could either be powered by an AGN or by an ultra-compact starburst. The ratio of the SF surface density ( _ SFR ) to the gas surface density ( _ H_2 ) indicates that SF is suppressed by a factor of 50 compared to normal star-forming galaxies if all gas is forming stars, and 150 for the outskirt (98 ) dense molecular gas if the central region is is powered by an ultra-compact starburst. The AGN-driven bulk outflow could account for this extreme suppression by hindering the fragmentation and gravitational collapse necessary to form stars through a process of turbulent injection.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1266 has mismatched molecular line profiles, a massive molecular outflow, unusually high HCN emission, and likely spatially varying or multiple molecular components.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 1266, NGC 3607 and NGC 4526, have line profiles inconsistent with for all or almost all of their other lines. For NGC 1266 (best fit by a Gaussian), the FWHM is significantly greater in its , and lines and is significantly narrower in both lines (further discussed in Section 4.3). alatalo11 find that NGC 1266's and line profiles are best fit by two nested Gaussians (with a shared centre). We thus perform such a nested two-Gaussian fit for the other molecular lines of NGC 1266, but find that the two-Gaussian fit does not result in a significantly lower ^ 2 value in these other lines.

As mentioned previously, NGC 1266 stands apart in many ways from the other galaxies. It has a massive molecular outflow, discovered during the IRAM 30m survey of the galaxies alatalo11 . Here we note its extremely high HCN/ ratio of 2.7, higher than most starbursts and Seyfert nuclei found in the literature.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1266 as a nearby lenticular galaxy with a massive molecular outflow, compact H I absorption, and a VLBA radio core consistent with an AGN driving the outflow.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266 is a nearby (D = 29.9 Mpc; temi+09 ) lenticular galaxy originally studied as part of the multiwavelength survey of 260 early-type galaxies currently being compiled by the \ collaboration cappellari+11 . The presence of broad wings requiring a two-component Gaussian fit in the CO(2--1) spectrum from the Institut de Radioastronomie Millim e trique (IRAM) 30-m telescope was the first sign of the exceptional nature of NGC 1266 young+11 . Follow-up millimeter observations performed with the Combined Array for Research in Millimeter Astronomy (CARMA) and the Submillimeter Array (SMA) suggested that NGC 1266 harbors a massive molecular outflow alatalo+11 . SMA imaging revealed the presence of redshifted and blueshifted lobes, which are coincident with H from the Spitzer Infrared Nearby Galaxy Survey (SINGS; kennicutt+03 ), 1.4 GHz continuum baan+06 , X-ray thermal bremsstrahlung alatalo+11 , and outflowing ionized and atomic gas emission davis+12 .

Our high spatial resolution H I absorption data reveal compact absorption against the radio continuum core co-located with the putative AGN, and the presence of a blueshifted spectral component re-affirms that gas is indeed flowing out of the system. Our VLBA observations at 1.65 GHz reveal one continuum source within the densest portion of the molecular gas, with a diameter d < 8 mas (1.2 pc), a radio power P_ rad = 1.48 10 ^ 20 W Hz ^ 1 , and a brightness temperature T_ b > 1.5 10 ^7 K that is most consistent with an AGN origin. The radio continuum energetics implied by the compact VLBA source, as well as archival VLA continuum observations at lower spatial resolution, further support the possibility that the AGN in NGC 1266 could be driving the molecular outflow.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1266 as a nearby lenticular galaxy whose AGN powers a massive molecular outflow and whose X-ray spectrum requires multiple components.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266: A nearby lenticular galaxy, harbouring an AGN which powers a massive MO detected in this source which harbours an AGN 2015ApJ...798...31A . Apart from extensive analysis of Chandra and XMM data, a multiwaveband study was carried out by the authors, weher they detected a soft emission from starburst, a powerlaw and Fe K line from the AGN. The intrinsic absorbtion column density estimated for this source from IR studies of 2015ApJ...798...31A is =3 10^ 24 , almost 3 orders of magnitude higher than that found using X-ray studies. Supression of Star formation in this S.B galaxy is studied by 2015ApJ...798...31A .

There is observation of this source but there is no published study. In our study we found that this source has a complex spectrum which required a soft thermal component ( APEC ) along with a warm absorber, a neutral intrinsic absorber, and soft X-ray emission lines at 1.48 and 1.85 in the observer's frame. The Fe K line was not detected due to poor SNR.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1266 as a lenticular galaxy with nuclear outflow or obscured-starburst context and reports a tentative NH3 (3,3) detection near its systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: One galaxy (NGC 1266) is lenticular, one (NGC 3256) is an interacting pair of former spirals, while the remaining seven are spirals. Several of the galaxies (NGC 253, NGC 1068, NGC 1266, NGC 1808, and Circinus) are known to host massive molecular outflows from the nucleus 2013Natur.499..450B,2014AaA...567A.125G,2011ApJ...735...88A,2016ApJ...823...68S,1997ApJ...479L.105V . Many (NGC 253, NGC 1808, NGC 3256, NGC 4945, M83, Circinus) are currently starbursting 2015MNRAS.450L..80B,2017AaA...598A..55B,2008MNRAS.384..316L,1994ApJ...429..602M,2016ApJ...832..142Z , and NGC 1266 may host a dust-obscured starburst 2015ApJ...798...31A .

In NGC 1266, we tentatively detect only the (3,3) line with 3 confidence, with only upper limits on the strengths of the remaining lines. The (3,3) line consists of a single component at 2175.6 1.5 km s ^ 1 , close to the systemic LSRK velocity of the system (2157 km s ^ 1 ). The velocity-integrated main beam brightness temperature is T_ mb d = 0.34 0.17 K km s ^ 1 . We do note tentative evidence for the existence of a (2,2) line at the systemic velocity, but the feature bears little to no resemblance to the (3,3) line and is low S/N, so we do not claim it as a detection.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1266 as a shocked, molecular-gas-rich S0 AGN host whose CO excitation is shock dominated and whose nucleus is likely compact-starburst dominated.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266 is a lenticular galaxy (S0) hosting an active galactic nucleus (AGN), and known to contain a large amount of shocked gas.

We confirm that in the mid- and high- J regions ( J_ up = 4--13), the C-type shock ( v_ s = 25 km s ^ 1 , n_ H = 5 10^ 4 cm ^ 3 ) can reproduce the CO observations well. The galaxy spectral energy distribution (SED) is constructed and modeled by the code X-CIGALE and obtains a set of physical parameters including the star formation rate (SFR, 1.17 0.47 M _ yr ^ 1 ). Also, our work provides SFR derivation of [ C ii ] from the neutral hydrogen regions only (1.38 0.14 M_ yr ^ 1 ).

Previous studies have illusive conclusions on the AGN or starburst nature of the NGC 1266 nucleus. Our SED model shows that the hidden AGN in the system is intrinsically low-luminosity, consequently the infrared luminosity of the AGN does not reach the expected level. Archival data from NuSTAR hard X-ray observations in the 3--79 keV band shows a marginal detection, disfavoring presence of an obscured luminous AGN and implying that a compact starburst is more likely dominant for the NGC 1266 nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1266 as an ATLAS3D galaxy with an AGN-driven molecular outflow, compact nuclear star formation, no major-merger evidence, and imminent quenching.

quotes.quote: Summary: We detail the rich molecular story of NGC 1266, its serendipitous discovery within the ATLAS ^ 3D survey (Cappellari et al. 2011) and how it plays host to an AGN-driven molecular outflow, potentially quenching all of its star formation (SF) within the next 100 Myr . While major mergers appear to play a role in instigating outflows in other systems, deep imaging of NGC 1266 as well as stellar kinematic observations from SAURON , have failed to provide evidence that NGC 1266 has recently been involved in a major interaction. The molecular gas and the instantaneous SF tracers indicate that the current sites of star formation are located in a hypercompact disk within 200 pc of the nucleus (Fig. 1; SF rate 2 M_ yr ^ 1 ).

On the other hand, tracers of recent star formation, such as the H absorption map from SAURON and stellar population analysis show that the young stars are distributed throughout a larger area of the galaxy than current star formation. As the AGN at the center of NGC 1266 continues to drive cold gas out of the galaxy, we expect star formation rates to decline as the star formation is ultimately quenched. Thus, NGC 1266 is in the midst of a key portion of its evolution and continued studies of this unique galaxy may help improve our understanding of how galaxies transition from the blue to the red sequence (Alatalo et al. 2011).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1266 as an S0 comparison object with a steep SED, high dust/stellar ratio, and likely central dust heating from an active nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266 and NGC 1316 are both S0s, and as we shall show later, they have similar masses. Nevertheless, they have very different dust/stellar flux ratios. In addition, they are among the galaxies that, before Herschel , did not have any far-IR data beyond 160 m.

Looking at another pair of S0s, NGC 1266 and NGC 1377 have particularly high dust/stellar ratios. For NGC 1266, we believe this could be due to an active nucleus (see Dale et al. 2007; Smith et al. 2007) which could be heating dust in the central region; however, this galaxy is likely an exception, as galaxies in the sample were selected not to have luminous AGN (Kennicutt et al. \ 2003).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1266 as an S0 galaxy with compact, highly excited molecular gas whose high-J CO and water emission require shocks and resemble ULIRG ISM conditions.

quotes.quote: We investigate the far infrared spectrum of NGC 1266, a S0 galaxy that contains a massive reservoir of highly excited molecular gas. Using the SPIRE-FTS, we detect the ^ 12 CO ladder up to J=(13-12), [ C 1 ] and [ N 2 ] lines, and also strong water lines more characteristic of UltraLuminous IR Galaxies (ULIRGs). The ^ 12 CO line emission is modeled with a combination of a low-velocity C-shock and a PDR. Shocks are required to produce the H _2 O and most of the high-J CO emission. Despite having an infrared luminosity thirty times less than a typical ULIRG, the spectral characteristics and physical conditions of the ISM of NGC 1266 closely resemble those of ULIRGs, which often harbor strong shocks and large-scale outflows.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 1266 as a post-starburst galaxy with a prominent double-lobed extended narrow-line region, shock-like emission near its dust lane, nitrogen-rich lines, and galaxy-wide outflows.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266: This galaxy shows an intermediate-age stellar spectrum, suggesting that it is post-starburst. It has a prominent ENLR with a complex, dynamically active, double-lobe morphology extending over 20 arcsec at PA = 0^ , roughly orthogonal in PA to the underlying disk. The southern lobe of the ENLR is blueshifted, and both lobes have large velocity broadening. The emission spectrum appears more shock-like close to the nuclear dust lane. The [ N 1 ] 5158,5200 doublet reaches an extraordinary strength here, surpassing \ and rivaling 5007 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1266 as an outflowing target with a high integrated broad-component density, while spatial maps show low density at the outflow location.

quotes.quote: Three of the targets, namely NGC 1068, NGC 1266 and NGC 2110, are classified as having an ionized outflow based on the presence of broad components in the 5007 profile of the S7 data. The broad components of the profiles in the three targets show fast outflows with FWHM > 500 km/s compared to the other targets which require only narrow components with FWHM < 200 km/s (See Sect. sect3.1 for more details on emission line fitting).

For the broad outflowing component in NGC 1068, NGC 1266 and NGC 2110, we get a density value of 726 90, 1120 295 and 237 78 cm ^ 3 respectively. The spectrum showing these broad components are shown in Fig. fig:sii_spectrum_outflow . Therefore, from the integrated spectrum analysis, the outflowing component has a significantly higher density compared to the non-outflowing components.

In fact for targets like NGC 1266, the density at the outflow location traced by is less than 50 cm ^ 3 throughout which further reinforces the need for cautiousness while interpreting results obtained from integrated spectrum analysis.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1266 as a lenticular KINGFISH galaxy with high star formation, shock-excited molecular gas in an outflow, and measurable radial dust SEDs.

quotes.quote: Some of the highest SFRs in the KINGFISH sample are found in the lenticulars (e.g., NGC 1482, NGC 1377, and NGC 1266); Such intense star-formation activity is not particularly unusual in S0 galaxies e.g., amblard14 , although these galaxies are not typical early-type systems. NGC 1377 is a nascent starburst with exceptionally warm dust and a deep silicate absorption feature at 9.7 \ vader93,laureijs00,roussel06 , and NGC 1266 has shock-excited molecular gas entrained in a molecular outflow e.g., alatolo11,pellegrini13 .
We show an example of each morphological type: an elliptical, NGC 584; a lenticular NGC 1266; a spiral NGC 6946; and a dwarf irregular Holmberg II (the latter three are found in Fig. fig:appradial ).
We are able to measure dust SEDs in NGC 1266, a lenticular SB0, out to 2 , and in NGC 584, an E4, to 1.6 .

sources.summary: NGC 1266 is presented as a nearby low-luminosity AGN where a kpc-scale radio jet drives multiphase outflow and turbulence-linked quenching.

quotes.quote: In Figure  fig:NGC1266_sim , we present our VLA and ngVLA simulations of a jetted low-luminosity AGN with properties similar to NGC 1266, an example of a relatively ``normal,'' nearby ( D = 29.9  Mpc) galaxy with strong evidence for AGN-driven feedback produced by its low-power ( L_ 1.5 GHz 10^ 22  W Hz ^ 1 ), kpc-scale radio jet. NGC 1266 hosts a multi-phase outflow in conjunction with a reduced star formation efficiency in the vicinity of the AGN. A number of studies over the past several years support a scenario in which the outflow is driven at least in part by the injection of turbulent energy from the radio jet alatalo+11, alatalo+14, alatalo+15, davis+12, nyland+13 . This galaxy thus highlights the importance of turbulence-driven quenching caused by AGN feedback associated with a low-power jet hosted by a galaxy with a stellar mass of (M_ * / M _ ) 11.56 , which is comparable to that of the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 1266 as a nearby post-starburst system with unusual dense gas, a possible embedded AGN, and a molecular outflow depleting nuclear gas.

quotes.quote: However, different behavior is observed for the more nearby systems NGC 5195 Kohno2002,Matsushita2010,Alatalo2016a and NGC 1266 Alatalo2015 . Observations of NGC 5195 are complicated by its on-going merger with M51, but is observed to have a star formation efficiency similar to normal galaxies. NGC1266 has a high HCN/CO ratio, which may be due to an embedded AGN Juneau2009 .

Alatalo2011 observed a molecular outflow in the post-starburst galaxy NGC 1266, which is rapidly ( <100 Myr) depleting the gas in the nuclear region of this galaxy. However, much of this gas will not be able to escape the galaxy Alatalo2015 , with an escape rate only 2 M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1266 as a local post-starburst comparison whose X-ray emission is extended thermal plasma linked to an outflow, while its AGN is obscured and faint.

quotes.quote: This concern emanates from the much deeper 150 ks observation observation undertaken on the local post-starburst galaxy NGC 1266 NGC1266 is a specifically interesting comparison for SPOGs because it has optical emission line characteristics that would have been selected against by classical post-starburst selection criteria but not the SPOGs selection criteria alatalo16a_spog1 . ( alatalo15 ; Lanz et al. in prep.). The majority of NGC 1266's X-ray emission, while concentrated within a few arcseconds of the AGN, is clearly extended, soft, best modeled as a thermal plasma, and coincident with a multiphase outflow. Its AGN appears to be both highly obscured and relatively low in luminosity. As such, NGC 1266 is a clear example of a post-starburst galaxy whose X-ray emission is not dominated by power-law emission.

sources.summary: The paper applies its graphical infrared-color procedure to NGC 1266, favoring a small PAH fraction and finding parameters consistent with earlier best-fit dust-model values.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266: The low value of P_ 7.9 0.044 can only be reproduced in Figure fig:IRAC4/MIPS band ratios by models with small values of . Of the values of for which spectra have been computed, =0.47 is preferred. Turning now to Figure fig:MIPS band ratios a, we find that 12.5 and 0.029 . The values of , , and found by this graphical exercise are in good agreement with the best-fit values of =0.47 , =12 , and =0.029 Draine+Dale+Bendo_etal_2006 .

sources.summary: The paper gives NGC1266 a low-temperature molecular-gas model, notes high surface density despite low SSFR, and predicts uncertain SLED turnover positions and kinetic temperatures.

quotes.quote: The kinetic temperatures obtained for the best-fit models are either low ( T_ K =10 20 K for IC1024, NGC1222, NGC1266, NGC2764, NGC3032, NGC3665, NGC4150, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC4694, NGC4710 and NGC5866), intermediate ( 30 70 K for IC0676, NGC3607, PGC058114 and UGC09519) or high ( 120 K for NGC6014 and 150 K for NGC7465).

In addition, high CO column densities may not be totally unexpected, as suggested by our CO interferometric maps ( Alat13 , hereafter Paper XVIII), where high surface densities are observed despite low SSFRs (as high as 10^ 4 M_ pc ^ 2 in NGC1266).

A similar behaviour is observed for NGC1266, NGC4694, NGC4710, NGC7465 and PGC058114, with a predicted SLED turnover position increment of 2 or 3 and a kinetic temperature increase by a factor of 3 to 6 within 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1266 as a red-sequence outlier with strong absorption, possible recent shutdown of star formation, and internal reddening from dust.

quotes.quote: The only notable differences are two outliers in the ( ) -- \ relation, NGC 1266 and UGC 09519, which are both red sequence galaxies in \ colours but have strong \ absorption. They might be experiencing a recent shutdown in star formation activity, as an abrupt shutdown would show up in the NUV flux before the \ line strength see also katey1266sf . These galaxies are also known to have internal reddening from dust, and colour images are presented in DRpaper .

sources.summary: NGC 1266 is presented as a comparison disk galaxy with possible AGN-driven molecular outflows, low inferred AGN fraction, and enhanced warm CO likely driven by shocks.

quotes.quote: Three are from the references listed above: M 82, an archetypical starburst; NGC 1266, a disk galaxy with possible AGN-driven molecular outflows (Alatalo et al. 2011); and NGC 1068, a well-known Seyfert galaxy with its CO line emission detected up to J = 30 (Hailey-Dunsheath et al. 2012) and f_ AGN 50 (Telesco Decher 1988).
In both NGC 1266 and 3C 293, our estimated f_ AGN < 10 , based on [ O iv ] / [ Ne ii ] from Dudik, Satyapal Marcu (2009) or Ogle et al. (2010).
Indeed, both NGC 1266 and 3C 293, where the warm CO emission might be enhanced respectively by the AGN- and radio jet-driven shocks, show a higher R_ midCO in Fig. 4.

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 1266 from its comparison sample because its nuclear star-forming knots dominate the X-ray emission and no extended hot gas is detected.

quotes.quote: NGC 1266 appears to have no extended hot gas emission. We note that there are clearly three star-forming knots close to the nucleus of the galaxy, and these dominate the X-ray emission, but we detect no X-ray emission beyond these. Thus, we conservatively choose to remove NGC 1266 from our analyses.

gmw_00000004.name: B1608+656
gmw_00000004.overview: B1608+656 is a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:2401.04475] and post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:1112.0005, arXiv:1503.02074] whose emission is quadruply lensed [arXiv:1112.0005, arXiv:1503.02074] by a pair of interacting early-type galaxies [arXiv:1112.0005, arXiv:1503.02074]. The lensing system is embedded in a low-mass galaxy group [arXiv:1112.0005, arXiv:1409.1326] and features a dust lane between the two merging deflectors [arXiv:1102.3433, arXiv:astro-ph/0408338].

sources.summary: The paper describes B1608+656 as a group lens with a nearby second lens galaxy, dust lane, elongated mass map, and problematic stellar-mass fraction from blending.

quotes.quote: B1608 resides in the middle of a galaxy group with 8 other group members according to . The photometry shows an object close to the main galaxy, which constitutes a second lens galaxy. This is confirmed by the reconstructed mass map (Fig. fig16 ) as it predicts a conspicuously elongated mass distribution towards NE. Images also show a prominent dust lane between the two galaxies.

The galaxy B1608 shows an unreasonably high stellar-mass fraction for radii .75 (e.g. left panel in top row of Fig. fig:mov1 ). To take proper account of the light distribution we fit both the brightest galaxy and its merging companion with Sersic profiles, but we only use the information of the light profile of the brightest galaxy for the computation of stellar mass. The enclosed mass values are thus taken with respect to the center of the brightest galaxy. As a consequence of the degeneracy between the two Sersic profiles the central region of the light profile is modeled rather poorly.

sources.summary: The paper confirms CLASS B1608+656 quadruply lensed-system delays with coherent multi-curve tests, retaining values near 31.6, 35.7, and 77.5 days.

quotes.quote: CLASS B1608+656 Light curves for this quadruply lensed system were first analysed by 1999ApJ...527..498F and subsequently improved in 2002ApJ...581..823F by adding more data. Using three observing seasons, they published time delays of t_ BA = 31.5_ 1 ^ +2 , t_ BC = 36.0 1.5 , and t_ BD = 77.0_ 1 ^ +2 days. Their analysis is based on a simultaneous fit to data from the three seasons but treats the curves only in pairs.

We performed several tests on these light curves, which are shown in Fig. fig:B1608 : first taking into account only the first and the third season separately (the second season not presenting useful structure), then all data for the three seasons simultaneously, using the three and four curve version of our method as described in Section sec:method . This enables us to impose coherence between the pairs of time delays, which was not done by 2002ApJ...581..823F . The results, as illustrated in Fig. B1608_histo_3C_tout , confirm the previously published values, within the error bars, of t_ BA = 30.2 0.9 , t_ BC = 36.2 1.1 , and t_ BD = 76.9 2.3 days.

The MD method entirely confirms these results of t_ BA = 32.9 2.9 , t_ BC = 35.2 2.5 , and t_ BD = 78.0 3.7 days with another indication for t_ AC 2.5 days. Combining both methods results in the time delays of t_ BA = 31.6 1.5 , t_ BC = 35.7 1.4 , and t_ BD = 77.5 2.2 days.

sources.summary: The paper models B1608+656 as a fold-caustic quasar lens where two early-type galaxies at z=0.630 fit the four images, fluxes, and time delays with an SIE-plus-shear model.

quotes.quote: B1608+656 The fold caustic lens B1608+656 consists of three bright images A, B, C and one faint image D. A source quasar at z_S=1.394 is lensed by two early-type galaxies G1 and G2 at z_L=0.630 myers1995,fassnacht1996 . The measured ellipticity and the position angle of near-IR light distribution are e=0.45 0.01 and _e=73.50 0.40(^ ) for G1 and e=0.55 0.01 and _e=-81.10 0.20(^ ) for G2 sluse2012 . The lens galaxy G1 belongs to a low-mass group of eight members fassnacht2006 . We use the astrometry of lensed images and the centroid of G1 and G2 in sluse2012 .

The fluxes and time delays between these four images are based on radio-wavelength monitoring with the Very Large Array at 8.5 GHz fassnacht2002 . Time delay between image A and B is denoted as t_ A t_ B = t_ BA . All the observed data are fitted well by an SIE(for G1)+ES(for environment)+SIE(for G2) model ^2_ tot / dof =1.6 though the best-fitting ellipticities e( G1 )=0.621, e( G2 )=0.759 are somewhat larger than the observed values in sluse2012 .

sources.summary: The paper applies its distance method to B1608+656, using image geometry and time-delay data, then estimating distance precision and showing velocity-dispersion uncertainty dominates.

quotes.quote: B1608+656 Figure fig:B1608+656 shows the image configuration of B1608+656 suyu/others:2009 . The information on image configuration is important as our formula applies only to a circularly symmetric case. Thus, the only image pairs we can use in this paper are the ones that are on the opposite sides of the lens center.

The data of B1608+656 are mostly from ref. , but the image positions are calculated from the data given in ref. , the time delays are from ref. , and the redshifts are from refs. . For this system, the origin of the coordinates is set at the image A.

In figure fig:dsig_dda , we show the expected fractional uncertainty in D_A(EL) to B1608+656 as a function of the uncertainty in the velocity dispersions, . The at the sweet-spot radius measured with 260 7 km/s corresponds to ^2 measured with 5 precision. This yields D_A(EL) with 7 precision, after marginalizing over velocity anisotropy.

sources.summary: The review describes B1608 + 656 as a CLASS radio lens with precise quad time delays, environmental corrections, and cosmological-distance constraints from detailed modeling.

quotes.quote: Monitoring of the CLASS lens B1608 + 656 in the radio with the Very Large Array enabled the breakthrough time delay measurements of Fas++02 . In its first season, this program yielded measurements of all three time delays in this quadruple image system with precision of 6--10 Fas++99 ; with the variability of the source increasing over the subsequent two seasons, Fas++02 were able to reduce this uncertainty to 2--5 . Such high precision was the result of a dedicated campaign which consisted of 8-month seasons, with a mean observation spacing of around 3 days. The light curves were calibrated to 0.6 accuracy.

The analysis of B1608 + 656 by Suy++10 explicitly took into account the weak lensing effects of external structures. Such a correction had been suggested by Fas++06b , who identified 4 galaxy groups along the line of sight in a spectroscopic survey of the B1608 + 656 field; the authors estimated that these groups could, if left unaccounted for, bias any inferred Hubble constant high by around 5 , an amount consistent with more general theoretical predictions Bar96,K+Z04 .

In Figure fig:current-constraints we reproduce the current constraints on cosmological parameters, from the two best-measured systems, B1608 + 656 and RXJ1131 Suy++14 . When this figure was made, the available precision from just these two lenses was about the same as that from SDSS DR7 Baryonic Acoustic Oscillations PercivalEtal2010 or the ``Constitution'' set of Type Ia supernovae HickenEtal2009 .

sources.summary: The paper describes B1608+656 as a two-galaxy interacting lens whose disturbed isophotes, dust, and uncertain dark-matter assignment complicate lens modeling and H0 estimates.

quotes.quote: In B1608+656, however, the lens galaxies show the heavily disturbed isophotes typical of galaxies undergoing a disruptive interaction. How one should model such a system is unclear. If there was once dark matter associated with each of the galaxies, how is it distributed now? Is it still associated with the individual galaxies? Has it settled into an equilibrium configuration?

The lens B1608+656 consists of two interacting galaxies, and, as we discussed in sec:data , this leads to a greatly increased parameter space. Fassnacht et al. (2002) used SIE models for the two galaxies to find H_0=61-65 , depending on whether the lens galaxy positions are taken from the H band or I band lens HST \ images (the statistical errors are negligible). The position differences are probably created by extinction effects from the dust in the lens galaxies.

sources.summary: CLASS1608+656 has strong radio time delays, but its interacting lens galaxies and dust lane make dark-matter modeling crucial and nontrivial.

quotes.quote: The system with the best time delays is CLASS1608+656. have measured four beautiful radio lightcurves. All four time series faithfully reproduce the many bumps and wiggles. The multiple delays in this case are good enough to provide meaningful additional constraints to the deflections and distortions. But modeling the system is not straightforward.

As with HE0230-2130, there are two lensing galaxies (figure fig:q1608 ) that appear to be interacting. A dust lane encircles them both. Again the question of how the dark matter might be distributed looms as crucial.

sources.summary: The paper finds CLASS 1608+656 requires significant additional shear; its lensed source is a radio galaxy and its image geometry deviates strongly from pure elliptical-potential expectations.

quotes.quote: The image configurations of these two systems, HST 12531--2914 and CLASS 1608+656 are shown in Figs. 1 and 2, respectively. For a pure elliptical potential, the lensing galaxy position must lie on the hyperbolic-like curves in these figures. Both systems show a significant deviation these curves, suggesting the presence of a significant off-axis shear (cf. W96).

In contrast the lensed source in CLASS 1608+656 is a radio galaxy and not a quasar. It is conceivable that the small separations or the extended source size make the position measurements more difficult and the errors on their positions are under-estimated. We therefore artificially enlarged their errors by a factor of 2, and recomputed their statistical significance. CLASS 1608+656 remains 5 significant while the significance for HST 12531--2914 has dropped to 1.7 . The exceptional nature of CLASS 1608+656 can also be seen from the ratio of the distances between the images C and D to A and B, _ CD / _ AB 1.0 (for HST 12531--2914 _ CD / _ AB 0.73).

sources.summary: The paper treats B1608+656 as a fold lens whose two lens galaxies make single-galaxy models inadequate and whose AC and AD image-pair time delays are strongly anomalous.

quotes.quote: We also find evidence for time delay anomalies in larger-separation image pairs in the fold lenses, B1608+656 and WFI 2033 4723, and the cusp lens RX J0911+0551.

All of the differential time delays are known for the fold lens 1608 Fassnacht-1608-tdel . Because there are two galaxies inside the Einstein angle, this lens is not necessarily expected to be well described by our models. Indeed, it is extremely hard for single-galaxy models (even with shear) to reproduce the d_1 and d_2 values for the image pair BD: we find only one mock image pair that matches the observed values.

Among the other image pairs, the fold pair AC and the distant pair AD are anomalous in terms of their scaled time delays. All of these anomalies are very strong; in fact, the AD scaled time delay and its 3 values have P-values that are strictly zero, meaning there are no matching mock lenses whose time delays are more extreme than the observed values. We note that these anomalies do not necessarily reveal CDM substructure, because the presence of two lens galaxies makes the lens potential more complicated than we have allowed for here.

sources.summary: The paper describes B1608+656 as a two-galaxy merging lens system with measured time delays, a group environment, and several possible line-of-sight groups.

quotes.quote: B1608+656 (hereafter 1608 ) was first reported by 1995ApJ...447L...5M . It consists of two merging galaxies. The main galaxy is an early-type galaxy which is disrupted by a smaller, probably late-type galaxy 2003ApJ...584..100S . The system has measured time delays 2002ApJ...581..823F . The environment has been analysed and a group of 8 (9 if the merging galaxies are counted individually) member galaxies was identified 2006ApJ...642...30F .
However, no significant X-ray emission was detected from the surrounding group 2005ApJ...625..633D . The data seem to indicate four other groups along the line-of-sight.

sources.summary: The paper models B1608+656 as a quadruple gravitational lens and shows point-source constraints alone allow a wide range of Hubble-constant predictions.

quotes.quote: We apply elliptical isothermal mass models to reproduce the point source properties, i.e. image positions, flux density ratios and time delay ratios, of the quadruple lens B1608+656.

Relative positions, flux ratios and time delays of the 4 images in B1608+656 have been presented here by Fassnacht (1999) and references therein ( cf Table  obs ). Koopmans Fassnacht (1999) have concluded H _ = 59^ +7 _ 6 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 within the context of a family of parametrized, isothermal models. Here, we investigate whether a larger set of models allows a wider range of Hubble constants.

A variety of parametrized models can reproduce the point source properties of B1608+656. This precludes an accurate determination of H _ . To break the degeneracy, extra constraints, associated with the extended emission of the source, have to be incorporated.

sources.summary: The paper uses B1608+656 as a case study, describing precise time delays, lens modeling with an Einstein ring and velocity dispersion, and a nearby group contribution to H_0.

quotes.quote: The B1608+656 lens system remains the only four-image system for which all three independent time delays have been measured to high precision (Fassnacht et al. \ 2002). New modeling incorporates the Einstein ring seen in HST/WFPC2 images as well as the stellar velocity dispersion of the lensing galaxy. The new model leads to a value of H_0 = 75^ +7 _ 6 km/s/Mpc (Koopmans et al. \ 2003).
