Furthermore, a 20-orbit multi-band observation of the system with the Advanced Camera for Surveys on HST has been acquired.

To examine the effect of the group on the value of H_0 derived from B1608+656 we must measure the group properties. As a simple first approximation, we describe the group as a singular isothermal sphere. The velocity dispersion measured from the confirmed group members is 185 63 km/s, which corresponds to a convergence of = 0.22/ .

sources.summary: The paper treats B1608+656 as a core-dominated lens with a second galaxy, whose observed fold ratios generally match isothermal model expectations.

quotes.quote: B1608+656 has a core-dominated source with a complex lensing structure in the form of a second galaxy. claim that B1608+656 is simple compared to other lens systems because they were able to reproduce the image configuration using a single, elliptical lens galaxy in their model. The ellipticity of the lens in their models turns out to have been a sufficiently accurate recreation of the combination of lensing galaxies in the real system.

KGP predicts R_ fold to be in an unusually small range, from 0.3 to 0.43. Observed R_ fold values, which range from 0.327 to 0.516, match up well Myers/Fassnacht:1995, Fassnacht/Pearson:1999, Snellen/deBruyn:1995, Fassnacht/Xanthopoulos:2002 . Our estimate for a SIS+ES is R_ fold =0.361 , and for PS+ES is R_ fold =0.577 , which is higher than both observations and other predictions, suggesting a more isothermal mass distribution.

sources.summary: The paper treats B1608+656 as an early-type galaxy lens with a companion and active-galaxy source, using published kinematic and lensing data to infer its density slope.

quotes.quote: B1608+656 B1608+656 mye95,sne95,fas96 consists of an early-type galaxy lens with a small companion at z_ l =0.63. The source is an active galaxy at z_ s =1.394. In koo03 , the lensing morphology and time delays of the images were used to constrain the lensing potential.

This was subsequently refined by incorporating kinematic information in suy10 , which allowed them to break the degeneracies between density slope and Hubble constant that would otherwise afflict the problem wuc02 . Their complete analysis, assuming Jaffe or Hernquist luminous densities, gives =2.08 0.03 . We use the same data as suy10 ( = 260 15 in a 0.42 ^ aperture, the effective radius is 0.6 ^ , and the Einstein radius of the main lens is 0.83 ^ ) to obtain =2.39 0.09 for the uncorrected estimate and =2.14 0.06 0.11 including aperture corrections.

sources.summary: The review identifies CLASS B1608+656 as a quad lens system with measured radio time delays, interacting lens galaxies, weak-lensing reconstruction, and foundational time-delay distance analysis.

quotes.quote: Despite this, it was with radio monitoring that Fas++02 measured all three independent time delays of the quad lens system CLASS B1608+656 with uncertainties of a few percent, making this the first system that was used for accurate cosmological distance measurements Suy++10, Jee19 .

Furthermore, the pixelated lens potential reconstruction of the complex interacting lens galaxies in the system B1608+656 showed only small (within 2 ) deviations from the power-law model, validating the usage of the power-law model for the lens mass distribution, up to a mass-sheet transformation.

Measurements of the shear field allows us to reconstruction . have demonstrated the weak lensing reconstruction for two strong lens systems, HE0435 1223 with negligible and B1608+656 with significant . As shown in Fig. fig:kext_pdf , the results of the reconstructed from weak lensing agree well with the obtained from galaxy number counts. Putting it all together: blind cosmological measurements By combining the time-delay measurements, the lens mass models, and characterisations of the external convergence along the line of sight, the H _0 Lenses in COSMOGRAIL's Wellspring H0LiCOW; Suyu17 collaboration, together with COSMOGRAIL Courbin11 and Strong-lensing at High Angular Resolution Programme SHARP; Chen19 , measured the distances and to 6 lensed quasar systems Wong20 . The technique for measuring the distances was developed and demonstrated on the first lens system B1608+656 Suy++10, Jee19 .

sources.summary: The paper describes CLASS B1608+656 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: CLASS B1608+656 (herein B1608) was the first lens discovered as part of CLASS browne03, myers03 . A z_s=1.394 post-starburst galaxy fassnacht96 is lensed into four images by a z_ l =0.6304 pair of interacting early-type galaxies. The stellar velocity dispersion of the main lens galaxy is _ =247 35 , and it has an E+A spectrum koopmans03 . The maximum separation between the images is 2.1 . B1608 is a time delay system with radio delays t_ BA =31.5 ^ +2 _ 1 days, t_ BC = 36.0^ +1.5 _ 1.5 days, and t_ BD = 77.0^ +2.0 _ 1.0 days fassnacht02 .

sources.summary: The paper treats B1608+656 as a four-image lens system of interacting lens galaxies, using its time delays and angular-diameter lens distance to test cosmological distance consistency.

quotes.quote: The B1608+656 is a four-image gravitational lens system formed by a pair of interacting lens Cd galaxies with z_l=0.6304 and an extended source at z_s=1.394 (see Fig. 2 in Jee, Komatsu Suyu 2015). This lens system has all three independent time delays between the images measured with errors of only a few percent, furnishing a great opportunity to measure the Hubble constant, H_0 . For instance, by fixing the cosmological parameters Suyu et al . (2010) found H_0 =70.6 3.1 km/s Mpc.

By considering an isotropic velocity dispersion and spherical symmetry, the D_ Aol 's obtained to the B1608+656 and RXJ1131-1231 systems were, respectively: 1485.7 208 Mpc and 813.33 105.75 Mpc, corresponding to a total uncertainty of 14 and 13 . The uncertainty in the inferred distance is dominated by the velocity dispersion error, while the effect of external convergence cancels out when dividing the time delays and velocity dispersion measurements.

sources.summary: The paper uses CLASS B1608+656 as a strong-lensing distance calibrator, discussing its foreground lens galaxy and effective-radius aperture for kinematic modeling.

quotes.quote: We show images of the two lensing systems, CLASS B1608+656 (hereafter B1608+656) and RXS J1131 1231 (hereafter RXJ1131 1231) in fig:lenses A-B, and schematics of the system configurations in fig:lenses C. The foreground galaxy that dominates the deflection is defined as the main lens, while the deflections caused by any other structure along the line of sight external to the lens is parameterized by the external convergence, _ ext . When the source brightness is variable, the arrival time difference between photons from different images (i.e. the time delay) can be measured. Physically, two effects contribute to the difference in photon arrival time: the projected gravitational potential of the enclosed mass, and the difference in geometric path lengths between images, which are summarized as the Fermat potential .
For B1608+656, R_ eff = 0.58'' , while R_ ap = 0.84'' 1'' , equivalent to 72 of the effective radius.

sources.summary: The paper presents B1608+656 as a time-delay lens whose main deflector consists of two merging galaxies modeled as a power law ellipsoid plus SIS component.

quotes.quote: B1608+656 This lens system was discovered by Myers++96 , with time delay measurements and early cosmographic inferences carried out by Fassnacht++02 and Koopmans++03 . The lens/source redshifts for this system are z_d = 0.45 , and z_s = 1.69 Fassnacht++96, Myers++96 . The longest delay in this system is 76 days, measured at 2 precision. This system is unique in that main deflector consists of two merging galaxies, although Suyu++09 show it is still well-described by a single power law profile. We follow Suyu++09 and model the larger object as a power law ellipsoid, and included smaller object as an SIS profile.

sources.summary: The paper includes B1608+656 as a TDCOSMO lens with CLASS discovery and source redshift, imaging and time-delay analyses, a 260 km/s Keck-LRIS velocity dispersion, and line-of-sight analysis.

quotes.quote: B1608+656: The discovery in the Cosmic Lens All-Sky Survey (CLASS) is presented by with the source redshift by . The imaging modeling is presented by and . The time-delay measurement is presented by . The velocity dispersion measurement of 260 km/s presented by is based on Keck-LRIS spectroscopy.
The line-of-sight analysis is presented by , based on galaxy number counts by .

sources.summary: The paper treats B1608+656 as a well-studied quad lens with four radio components, an Einstein ring, interacting lens galaxies, known redshifts, and lens-model degeneracies affecting Hubble-constant estimates.

quotes.quote: The well-studied quad, B1608+656 has four variable radio components arranged around an Einstein ring and labeled A, B, C, D (Fig. 1a). The scalar magnifications relative to B of A, C, D, at the same emission time, are 2, 1, 0.35 and the associated delays are 26, 33, 73 d respectively. The source and lens redshifts are known to be 0.63, 1.394 (Fassnacht, these proceedings and references therein). The lens comprises two interacting galaxies G1, G2.

This can be demonstrated for B1608+656 by exhibiting two different mass models that fit the four radio image positions and magnifications with reasonable accuracy as well as the ratios of the reported time delays and yet which yield Hubble constants of 60,100 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , respectively, (Surpi Blandford, these proceedings) This degeneracy may be broken when there is extended emission from an Einstein ring as it is then possible to match points with similar surface brightness.

sources.summary: The paper describes B1608+656 as a quad lens with measured independent time delays, two lensing galaxies, and large shear effects that strongly change inferred H0.

quotes.quote: B1608+656 This system is the first and up to now only quad for which all three independent time delays have been measured koopmans99i . This offers the unique possibility to apply our method to a system providing the complete set of constraints. HST images show a main lensing galaxy but also a weaker second galaxy between the four images jackson98 . We nevertheless apply the method to B1608+656, if only to see if the effect of a secondary lens can be detected in this way, e.g. \ by pretending there is a very large external shear. Data for the positions of the images and the main lensing galaxy were taken from koopmans99ii .

sources.summary: The paper describes CLASS B1608+656 as a quadruply lensed post-starburst radio galaxy system with two early-type lenses and multiple foreground groups.

quotes.quote: CLASS B1608+656 (j) . This lensed quasar, which is the core of a post-starburst radio galaxy Fassnacht1996 , was discovered by Myers1995 as part of the Cosmic Lens All-Sky Survey. This object is quadruply lensed by two early-type galaxies located at the same redshift. The main lens belongs to a low-mass group of redshift z_ g =0.631 that is composed of eight confirmed members. Moreover, three other groups are located in the foreground of the primary lens: they contain 10 confirmed members each and are respectively located at z_ g_ 1 =0.265 , z_ g_ 2 =0.426 and z_ g_ 3 =0.52 Fassnacht2006 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies B1608+656 as a radio-loud QSO with strong jet emission, measured millimetre continuum fluxes, and possible evidence for a longer active AGN history.

quotes.quote: Three of our sources, B1608+656, B1152+200, and B1600+434, have strong jet--dominated radio emission browne2003 .
B1608+656: this QSO at z = 1.394 has a continuum flux of 18.4 0.7 mJy at 94 GHz and 16.0 0.7 mJy at 110 GHz, a factor of two larger than the value of 8.1 0.4 mJy reported by .

Instead, it is likely that the cumulative effects of several episodes of AGN activity are necessary to significantly depress the gas fractions piotrowska2022 , and could indicate that the central AGN in B1608+656, B1152+200 and B1600+434 have been active for a longer period of time compared to the rest of the sample.

gmw_00000005.name: VI IZw 403
gmw_00000005.overview: VI IZw 403 is a nearby blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1808.08525, arXiv:1502.03443] located in the periphery of the M81 group [arXiv:1409.1239]. It features a kinematically disturbed disk [arXiv:1404.6252] with central starburst activity [arXiv:1910.04167] that hosts expanding H II shells [arXiv:1203.6213, arXiv:1808.08525] and a variable luminous X-ray binary [arXiv:1502.03443].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes VII Zw 403, linking its recent starburst age to shell kinematics and identifying an expanding shell between central bright HII regions.

quotes.quote: VII Zw 403 We present here the re-analyzed data first described in Lozinskaya2006 . An empty spot near the centre is caused by masking the trace of a bright parasitic reflex (see MoiseevEgorov2008 for details on the ghost reflex). Galaxy VII Zw 403 is one of the most well-described in the literature objects of our sample. According to Lynds1998 the age of the latest burst of star formation here is 4--10 Myr, which closely coincides with the estimates of the kinematic age of shells, associated with bright regions of star formation at least 3--4 Myr old, according to Lozinskaya2006 . On the other hand, the velocity dispersion of ionized gas in the HII regions themselves is small: <( _m=18 ).

The I- diagram confidently reveals an inclined lane (marked with green and orange) which, according to the model by MunozTunon1996 , must point to the presence of an expanding shell. The distribution of points on the map of the galaxy shows that this shell (VII Zw 403 1) is located between the bright HII regions in the centre of the galaxy and is adjacent to region 4 according to the numbering of Lynds1998 . The kinematic age of the shell coincides with the estimates by Lozinskaya2006 given above Note that the use of the approximation by the Voigt profile in our work allows making more accurate estimates of the expansion rate than the Gaussian approximation in Lozinskaya2006 .

sources.summary: The study finds UGC 6456 is kinematically disturbed and can be modeled as a disk with comparable rotation and radial motions instead of an unusual asymmetric warp.

quotes.quote: Seven galaxies in our sample have a kinematically disturbed disk: NGC 625, NGC 1569, NGC 4163, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, UGC 6456, and UGC 9128. For these objects, we used channels maps and PV-diagrams to estimate ( _ kin , _ kin ) , V_ sys , PA _ kin , and the rotation velocity in the outer parts. For the inclination, we assumed the optical value.

UGC 6456 has been previously studied by Thuan2004 and Simpson2011 . Simpson2011 derived a rotation curve using low-resolution (C+D array) observations. They assumed different values of the PA for the approaching and receding sides, which would imply an unusual, asymmetric warp starting within the stellar component (see their Fig. 13). Our 3D models show that the kinematics may be simply explained by a disk with V_ rot V_ rad _ 10 km s ^ 1 (see Fig fig:u6456 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents VII Zw 403 as a nearby blue compact dwarf with recent central star formation, H II shells, quiet ionized-gas kinematics, and no detected line-ratio correlation.

quotes.quote: Mrk 35 and VII Zw 403---Dwarf Galaxies with a Burst of Star Formation VII Zw 403 is one of the nearest blue compact dwarf galaxies with several episodes of recent star formation. The current outburst is located in the central kpc, where several compact OB-stars associations are identified and the associated H II shells that are immersed in the diffuse ionized gas see and references therein Egor2011 . The fields of velocities and velocity dispersions of the ionized gas in this galaxy were previously considered in Loz2006,Mois2012,Mois2015 , where a sufficiently quiet kinematics of the gas with a low level of peculiar velocities was noticed. The value is in the range of 15--40 .

The only galaxy in which we did not find a correlation between and characteristic line flux relations (or parameter) is VII Zw 403. The ongoing star-formation rate here is the lowest in our sample 0.015 M_ yr ^ 1 , Loz2006 . Apparently, for this reason, the contribution of shock waves to gas ionization is practically invisible.

sources.summary: The paper includes VII Zw 403 in a Chandra dwarf-starburst comparison and reports no diffuse X-ray detection, attributing the non-detection partly to short exposure, small volume, and low metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive comparison of the X-ray properties of a sample of eight dwarf starburst galaxies observed with Chandra (I Zw 18, VII Zw 403, NGC 1569, NGC 3077, NGC 4214, NGC 4449, NGC 5253, He 2--10). In Paper I we presented in detail the data reduction and analysis of the individual galaxies. For the unresolved X-ray sources we find the following: point sources are in general located close to bright \ regions, rims of superbubbles, or young stellar clusters.

No diffuse X-ray emission is detected in I Zw 18 and VII Zw 403. The integration times of both objects are the lowest of all the galaxies in the sample and the detection limits of the unabsorbed luminosities are of order 2-4 10^ 38 \ (see Paper I). The 5--10 times smaller size of I Zw 18 and VII Zw 403 (see Table tab:sample_props_opt ), however, reduces the volume of these galaxies up to three orders of magnitude. For this reason, the hot gas might be below the detection threshold if it has similar properties to that found in the rest of the sample.

sources.summary: The paper models VI IZw 403 as a BCD whose prolonged low-rate starburst drives a superbubble but probably will not eject new metals into the intergalactic medium.

quotes.quote: The recent history of star formation in VI IZw 403 is analyzed taking into account the dynamics of the starburst blown superbubble and the restrictions that follow from X-ray observations, and from our H data. Our results show that the starburst energizing VIIZw 403 is NOT coeval, but rather the star forming phase has lasted for more than 30Myr and at such a low pace that most probably the newly processed metals will not be ejected into the intergalactic medium. VII Zw403 is a BCD galaxy, in recent years considered in many discussions related to star formation histories and the possible impact of dwarf systems on the surrounding intergalactic medium.

It is an isolated system, however considered to be a member of M81 group. The X-ray observations (Papaderos et al. 1994; Fourniol 1997) added more interest to the system, as they revealed an extended kpc-scale region of diffuse X-ray emission. Several models have been investigated with the aim of matching the main observed parameters of VIIZw 403: The H luminosity ( L_ H = 1.8 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 ), the X-ray power ( L_ X = 2.3 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 ) and the size of the diffuse X-ray emitting region ( 1 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper uses archival HST data for the blue compact dwarf UGC 06456, resolves blue and red stellar populations, finds an obvious TRGB, and derives a 4.34 Mpc distance.

quotes.quote: From the archive data we used three 1400s exposures in F555W and three 1400s exposures in F814W of the blue compact dwarf galaxy (BCD) UGC 06456. We reduced these data as described in  2.2 for our own HST observations. The observations resolved the galaxy brilliantly into stars revealing a bright and knotted population of blue stars at its core and a smooth background of red stars.

The TRGB is extremely obvious and the maximum likelihood analysis finds the TRGB at I_ TRGB = 24.23 0.02 mag, as shown in Fig.  figure:ugc06456_halo_ccut_like , and the color of the TRGB is (V-I)_ 0 , TRGB = 1.46 0.02 mag. We thus take the metallicity of the red giant branch to be [ Fe/H ] = 1.62 0.05 . Using an extinction of A_I = 0.070 mag we find the distance modulus for UGC 06456 to be (m-M)_ = 28.19 0.04 (internal) 0.18 (systematic) mag and hence the heliocentric distance to be 4.34 0.07 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 6456 as a lopsided dwarf with a southern extension, Galactic-emission-affected H I data, and a peripheral location in the M81 group.

quotes.quote: Other galaxies, instead, show lopsided morphologies, characterized by minor asymmetries and/or extensions in the outer parts (NGC 2366, NGC 4068, NGC 4214, UGC 6456, UGC 9128, UGC 4483, and SBS 1415+437).

UGC 6456 has a disk that is slightly more extended to the South. The data are affected by Galactic emission, making the total map uncertain. Our results are in agreement with those of Simpson2011 . UGC 6456 lies in the periphery of the M81 group Karachentsev2005 .

sources.summary: UGC 6456 is described with double-peaked H I extended in several directions, gas aligned with faint optical emission, an offcenter main peak, and disturbed rotation-plus-radial motions.

quotes.quote: UGC 6456 (also known as VII Zw 403) From Thuan, Hibbard L evrier (2004; their Fig. 17, 18 and 20), and Lelli, Verheijan Fraternali (2014a, b; their Fig. 1, C.12, and Fig. 1, 2), from the VLA. The intermediate resolution (20 x 20 , 21.2 x 14.5 , 17.2 x 11 and 15 x 15 ) H 1 maps show double-peaked disk emission (PA _ HI 75 ^ ), extended to the Northwest, South and West. The H 1 emission does not align with the main optical emission (PA _ opt 115 ^ ), but does align with the faint extended emission (PA _ opt 75 ^ ). The main H 1 peak coincides, not with the center of the optical disk, but with the brightest star forming regions to the South in the optical image. The velocity field is disturbed, and can be modeled by a combination of rotation and radial motions. The kinematical axis (PA _ kin 90 ^ ) is vertical, with the rotation occurring along the H 1 major axis.

sources.summary: The paper examines VII Zw 403 as a nearby low-metallicity blue compact dwarf hosting a variable luminous X-ray binary relevant to early-galaxy X-ray heating.

quotes.quote: We examine the X-ray spectra of VII Zw 403, a nearby low-metallicity blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxy. The galaxy has been observed to contain an X-ray source, likely a high mass X-ray binary (HMXB), with a luminosity of 1.3-23 10^ 38  erg s ^ 1 in the 0.3-8  keV energy range. A new Suzaku observation shows a transition to a luminosity of 1.7 10^ 40  erg s ^ 1 [ 0.3-8  keV ] , higher by a factor of 7-130 .

Understanding the spectral evolution of HMXBs in early universe analogue galaxies, such as BCDs, is an important step in estimating their contribution to the heating of the intergalactic medium during the Epoch of Reionization.

sources.summary: The paper includes VII Zw 403 (UGC 6456) among DGS XMPs with CO upper limits and explains a discrepancy in adopted H I mass through different distances.

quotes.quote: The Dwarf Galaxy Survey (DGS; Madden et al. 2013) contains 18 sources in common with the original XMP sample, only three of which possess CO observations that yield H _2 mass upper limits (Cormier et al. 2014): SBS 0335-052, IZw 18 and VII Zw 403 (UGC 6456). The integrated measurements for these XMPs are included in the subsequent analysis, where _ H_2 (Spitzer, APEX Atacama Pathfinder Experiment , Mopra 22-m and IRAM Institut de Radioastronomie Millim etrique 30-m data), _ HI (see Madden et al. 2013 for details) and _ SFR (Spitzer data) are sampled within r_ dust 50 -- 90 or r_ CO 10 -- 30 (the dust and CO aperture, respectively).

Although there is no MPA-JHU SFR estimation for VII Zw 403, the adopted total HI mass (log(M _ HI /M _ ) 6.7; Filho et al. 2013 and references therein) is approximately an order of magnitude less than the value given in Cormier et al. (2014; log(M _ HI /M _ ) 7.5). Because it appears that the adopted HI data are the same (Thuan, Hibbard L evrier 2004), the difference in HI mass is due to different adopted distances (4.5 Mpc in Madden et al. 2013 versus 1.42 Mpc in Filho et al. 2013).

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 6456 as a centrally concentrated starburst with ionized bubbles, diffuse minor-axis emission, gas extending beyond neutral hydrogen, and velocity-dispersion peaks.

quotes.quote: UGC 6456 In Figure fig:ugc6456 , the central 1 kpc region in UGC 6456 is dominated by higher surface brightness \ emission that resolved into a number of bubbles, some of which appear to be superimposed. In this centrally concentrated starburst, the star formation activity has increased in the last 50 Myr, and the locations of the largest bubbles are also the sites of young BHeB stars McQuinn2012a . The diffuse \ emission lies along the northeast / southwest minor axis, perpendicular to the main stellar and gas disks.

sources.summary: The paper finds one unresolved X-ray source in VII Zw 403's optical body, cannot cross-identify it with HST data, and finds no extended diffuse X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations The ACIS-S3 Chandra observations of VII Zw 403 were performed with an integration time of 10 ks. One unresolved X-ray source is detected to coincide with the optical body of VII Zw 403 (see the upper right panel of Fig. fig:nodetect_allfig for an overlay, and Table tab:source_counts for coordinates, net source counts, and hardness ratios of the X-ray point source). Comparing our map to available HST /WFPC2 data, the X-ray source is located on the edge of the WFPC2 PC CCD, so unfortunately no cross-identification is possible. In contrast to the ROSAT observations, we neither find evidence for any extension of the discrete source, nor for any extended, diffuse X-ray emission in VII Zw 403 (the upper limit for diffuse X-ray emission is given in Table tab:plasma_limits ).

gmw_00000006.name: 3C 129
gmw_00000006.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 129 is a prototype narrow-angle-tail source [arXiv:astro-ph/0202255] characterized by bent radio jets [arXiv:astro-ph/0202255, arXiv:1610.02400] and a plume-like double-tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0202255]. It resides within the CIZA J0450.0+4501 galaxy cluster alongside the companion galaxy 3C 129.1 [arXiv:1412.5324].

sources.summary: The paper places 3C 129 inside a newly traced ZoA galaxy concentration, describing it as a head-tail radio source embedded with 3C 129.1 in the CIZA cluster environment.

quotes.quote: The most conspicuous is a ridge at about 160^ , v 6500 . Within this wall-like structure, two strong radio galaxies (3C 129 and 3C 129.1) are embedded which lie at the same distance as the ridge. They seem to form part of an X-ray cluster.

Their position and redshifts (6236 and 6655 km s ^ 1 respectively; ) confirm that they reside inside the galaxy concentration. These are the head-tail radio source (3C 129) and the double-lobed giant elliptical radio galaxy (3C 129.1). The presence of such radio sources with bent lobe morphology usually is indicative of a rich cluster environment.

The suspicion that 3C 129 and 3C 129.1 form part of a massive cluster was later substantiated through the identification of the X-ray cluster CIZA J0450.0+4501 ( ). The radio sources lie within the X-ray emission of the CIZA cluster and are at the same distance.

sources.summary: The paper maps 3C 129 as a narrow-angle-tail radio galaxy whose bow shock may revive fossil plasma, producing the steep-spectrum Crosspiece near its head.

quotes.quote: We present a new wide-field map of the radio galaxy 3C 129 and its companion galaxy 3C 129.1 at = 90 cm. We see a distinct steep-spectrum feature near the head of 3C 129, extending in a direction perpendicular to the radio tails. We propose that this Crosspiece might consist of fossil radio plasma, which has been re-energized by the compression of the bow shock of the supersonically moving galaxy 3C 129.
3C 129 shows prototypical narrow-angle-tail (NAT) morphology, with a plume-like double-tail extending nearly 30' at 90 cm. It has a total flux density of 5.3 Jy at 1400 MHz wb92 .

Here we propose that the bow shock of 3C 129 itself might have revived the radio emission of the Crosspiece. In order to explain the bending of 3C 129's jets, a velocity of 3C 129 with respect to the ICM of v_ gal = 500 - 3000 km s ^ 1 is assumed by various authors cm75,1995AJ....110...46D,ly00 .

sources.summary: The paper maps 3C 129 at low radio frequencies, finding jet spectral steepening and interpreting the Crosspiece as fossil radio plasma revived by the galaxy.

quotes.quote: We have mapped the head-tail radio galaxy, 3C 129 at 240 and 610 MHz using the GMRT and studied the detailed morphology and spectral index variations in this object. This is the first attempt to observe a sample of head-tail sources at low frequencies. We find weak spectral steepening as we go away from the head along the jet.

The VLA map of the strong, prototype head-tail source 3C 129 at 325 MHz shows a small perpendicular object, referred to as the Crosspiece, near the head of the galaxy. This feature has a steep spectrum and has been interpreted by Lane et al. (2002, AJ, 123, 2985) as a pre-existing fossil radio source that is revived due to 3C 129 ploughing through it. The relic source is compressed by the bow shock of 3C 129 causing it to radiate behind the shock front, producing the characteristic shape.

sources.summary: The paper links 3C 129 to a cluster X-ray halo and head-tail radio structure, with merged maps showing truncated halo emission affected by the cluster periphery.

quotes.quote: GRG J0448+4502 This source (3C 129) forms, together with 3C 129.1 (a compact source to the left in both panels of Fig. 4), a galaxy cluster showing an extended X-ray halo (Taylor et al. 2001). This halo centres on 3C 129.1 and extends out to the ``head'' of the head-tail structure of 3C 129. Outside of this halo, the most intense radio emission is visible in the merged map (lower panel of Fig. 4). High-resolution radio observations at higher frequencies performed by Taylor et al. (2001) revealed bright extended lobes forming the ``head'' of the source and extending further out in the northwest direction to form the ``tail'' structure visible in the NVSS image (upper panel of Fig. 4). The merged map shows a radio halo in the position of the source, truncated on its northern side.

sources.summary: The paper maps 3C 129 as a tailed radio galaxy, deriving its synchrotron-spectrum variation, radiative age, supersonic motion, and strong tail-aligned polarization.

quotes.quote: Total intensity images at an angular resolution of 2.9 were obtained for the tailed radio galaxy 3C 129 and for 13 more sources in the field, including 3C 129.1 at the galaxy cluster center. These data were used, in combination with literature data at lower frequencies, to derive the variation of the synchrotron spectrum of 3C 129 along the tail of the radio source. If the magnetic field is at the equipartition value, we showed that the lifetimes of radiating electrons result in a radiative age for 3C 129 of t_ syn 267 26 Myrs. Assuming a linear projected length of 488 kpc for the tail, we deduced that 3C 129 is moving supersonically with a Mach number of M=v_ gal /c_ s =1.47 .
The linear polarization measured for 3C 129 reaches levels as high as 70 in the faintest region of the source where the magnetic field is aligned with the direction of the tail.

sources.summary: The paper uses VLA observations of the bent-tailed radio galaxy 3C129 to show that PURIFY reconstructions reveal more apparent internal structure in its radio jets.

quotes.quote: The observation of the bent tailed radio galaxy 3C129 has a phase center of RA = 04h 45m 31.695s, DEC = +44 ^ 55 ^ 19.95 ^ (J2000), and was obtained from the NRAO archive. It was performed using the VLA with the project code AT0166, with two 50 MHz channels centered at 4.59 and 4.89 GHz. The observations were performed on the 25 ^ th of July 1994 in configuration B and 3 ^ rd of November 1994 in configuration C respectively. The total integration time was 79.7 minutes in configuration B and 15.8 minutes in configuration C.
For example, in reconstructed images of 3C129 the internal structure of the radio jets is much more apparent (Figure fig:3C129 ).

sources.summary: The appendix identifies 3C 129 as a CO(2-1) detection with no CO(1-0) detection, a 200 km/s FWHM velocity width, and no Hubble-image interaction signature.

quotes.quote: 3C 129: It is another example of CO(2-1) detection with an upper limit in the CO(1-0) line. The FWHM velocity width of this galaxy is 200 km/s. There is no hint of interaction in the Hubble image.

gmw_00000007.name: NGC 1275
gmw_00000007.overview: NGC 1275 is the central brightest cluster galaxy of the Perseus cluster [arXiv:2006.07631, arXiv:2405.13503], hosting the active radio source 3C 84 [arXiv:2006.07631, arXiv:2405.00097]. The galaxy is surrounded by a massive filamentary emission-line nebula [arXiv:1105.1735, arXiv:2311.16247] and exhibits recurrent nuclear jet activity [arXiv:1505.01575, arXiv:2005.08569].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1275 as the observed benchmark for cluster-core filaments, describing its extended nebula, filament kinematics, molecular gas reservoir, and uncertain power source.

quotes.quote: NGC 1275 at the centre of the Perseus cluster contains the best studied example of such a nebula hatch . Its filaments are typically 50-100 pc thick and up to 30 kpc long, and the majority of them are radial. presented kinematic data that rules out dynamical models of purely infalling filaments. The observed kinematic properties provide strong evidence that the filaments are not in gravitational free fall, because, if they were, their velocities would rise sharply towards the centre of the nebula heckman .

NGC 1275 contains a large reservoir of cold molecular gas that could fuel them. The filaments have morphologies similar to those of laminar jets, and their relatively smooth structures may place constraints on turbulent motions in the ICM.
The optical nebula around NGC 1275 emits 4.1 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 in H and N [ II ] . The nature of the power source remains unclear.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1275 as an unusual nearby Perseus radio galaxy observed by VERITAS after Fermi detection, with no VHE detection but evidence for a spectral deviation near 100 GeV.

quotes.quote: NGC 1275 (Perseus A, 3C 84) is a nearby ( z = 0.018 ) radio galaxy located in the center of the Perseus cluster. It is one of the most unusual early-type galaxies in the nearby Universe. Its radio emission is core dominated, but it also has strong emission lines. In addition, the emission line system shows a double structure, corresponding to both a high-velocity and a low-velocity system. The puzzling nature of NGC 1275 makes it difficult to definitively classify it in a standard AGN sub-class.

It has been recently detected in high energy rays by Fermi , with a flux between 100 MeV and 25 GeV described by a power law with photon index 2.17 . VERITAS observed the source between January and February 2009 for a total amount of good-quality data of 7.8 hours. Additional Fermi LAT data simultaneous to the VERITAS observational campaign have been analyzed, reporting a lower flux by a factor of 1.37 and a similar photon index 2.15 compared to the 2008 published Fermi LAT spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 1275 to Perseus-core X-ray bubbles, filaments, and an active nucleus whose influence dominates the disturbed inner cluster region.

quotes.quote: New depressions in X-ray surface brightness are discovered to the north of NGC 1275, which we interpret as old rising bubbles. They imply that bubbles are long-lived and do not readily breakup when rising in the hot cluster atmosphere. The existence of a 300 kpc long NNW--SSW bubble axis means there cannot be significant transverse large scale flows exceeding 100 .

A semi-circular cold front about 100 kpc west of the nucleus is seen. It separates an inner disturbed region dominated by the activity of the active nucleus of NGC 1275 from the outer region where a subcluster merger dominates.

Einstein X-ray imaging showed a dip in the X-ray emission to the NW of the nucleus of the central galaxy, NGC 1275, (Branduardi-Raymont et al 1981; Fabian et al 1981). ROSAT HRI data (B ohringer et al 1993) showed that this dip was an outer bubble in the hot X-ray emitting gas and resolved two inner bubbles coincident with the FRI radio source, 3C84 (Pedlar et al 1990). Churazov et al (2000) showed that the bubbles are inflated by the radio jets and rise buoyantly in the surrounding hot gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 84 as a Seyfert 2 with smoothly rising millimeter flux, double-peaked flux distributions, spectral flattening after 2007, red-noise spectra, and a year-scale lag.

quotes.quote: 3C 84 The 3-mm (1.3-mm) lightcurve of this Seyfert 2 galaxy varies smoothly during the entire monitoring time between 3.5 Jy ( 2 Jy) in 2002 and 11 Jy ( 7 Jy) in 2010. Since about 2004 the fluxes raise continuously with exponential rise time scales of 6 years ( 4 years). The flux distributions show somewhat complicated double-peak profiles that can be approximated as superpositions of three Gaussians peaking at 4 Jy, 5.5 Jy, and 9 Jy ( 2 Jy, 4 Jy, and 6 Jy). The spectral index is steep, with 0.9 (within errors) until about 2005 and then drops smoothly to 0.5 after 2007.

We find the flattest spectral index for the highest flux value; however, as this is based on a single data point that occupies a large range in flux, this observation does not establish a significant trend. The power spectra can be described as simple red-noise spectra, with powerlaw indices 0.7 for the 3-mm and 0.4 for the 1.3-mm lightcurve, respectively. The discrete correlation function computed from the 1.3-mm and 3-mm lightcurves finds a spectral time delay significantly different from zero. The most probable time delay is located at 270 ... -330 days, meaning that the 1.3-mm emission preceeds the 3-mm emission by almost one year.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBA monitoring of radio galaxy 3C 84, associating it with NGC 1275 and tracing compact lobes, sub-pc jet structure, and the brightening, sub-relativistic acceleration of component C3.

quotes.quote: The radio source 3C 84 is associated with the giant elliptical galaxy NGC 1275 ( z=0.0176 ). It is well known that 3C 84 has a pair of compact radio lobe structures on the central 10-pc scale 1994ApJ...430L..41V, 1994ApJ...430L..45W, 2000ApJ...530..233W, 2006PASJ...58..261A . Because of its proximity, it allows us to study the region within the central sub-pc scale using a Very Long Baseline Interferometer (VLBI). It is an ideal laboratory to investigate the formation mechanism of relativistic jet ultimately powered by super-massive black holes and the interaction between the jets and ambient matter in the galactic central regions.

We find that the new component ``C3'', previously reported by the observations with the VLBI Exploration of Radio Astrometry (VERA), was already formed in 2003. The flux density of C3 increases moderately until 2008, and then it becomes brighter rapidly after 2008. The radio core, C1, also shows a similar trend. The apparent speed of C3 with reference to the core C1 shows moderate acceleration from 0.10c to 0.47c between 2003 November to 2008 November, but is still sub-relativistic.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1275 as a radio-galaxy test case, fitting its Fermi spectrum while showing magnetic-field degeneracy, BLR scale compatibility, uncertain black-hole mass, and disk-absorption behavior.

quotes.quote: In , we presented a fit to the Fermi spectrum of the radio galaxy NGC 1275. We now show that there is a degeneracy of the magnetic field, both orientation and strength, if only the high energy output from the cascades is considered. Figure Degeneracy1 shows this effect for NGC 1275. In this plot, the external radiation field is parameterized through u_ ext = 5 10^ 2 erg cm ^ 3 with photon energy E_s = E_ Ly and R_ ext = 10^ 16 cm. This size scale is appropriate for low-luminosity AGN as observed in NGC 1275 e.g. kaspi07 , and the parameters combine to a BLR luminosity of L_ BLR = 4 R_ ext ^2 u_ ext = 1.9 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , in agreement with the observed value for NGC 1275.

The mass of the black hole in NGC 1275 is uncertain, and estimates range from a few times 10^6 M_ levinson95 to 10^8 M_ wilman05 . Assuming a characteristic fraction of 0.1 of the accretion-disk luminosity to be re-processed in the BLR, the accretion-disk luminosity may be estimated to be L_D 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . Figure diskabs shows the absorption depth due to the disk radiation field for L_D = 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 for the two possible extreme values of the black-hole mass, as a function of height z of the emission region above the accretion disk. It shows that for M_ BH = 10^6 M_ , the opacity drops below one at 10^3 r_g 10^ 14 cm from the black hole, while for M_ BH = 10^8 M_ absorption becomes negligible at 10^2 r_g 10^ 15 cm for primary rays of E_ = 1 TeV, and much earlier for lower-energy photons.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1275 as the Perseus brightest cluster galaxy, measuring FIR lines, dust and synchrotron SED components, and classifying it as a luminous infrared galaxy.

quotes.quote: Especially intriguing is the network of filaments that surround the brightest cluster galaxy, NGC 1275, previously imaged extensively in and CO. In this work, we report detections of far-infrared (FIR) lines, in particular, 158 , 63 , 122 , 145 and 88 , with Herschel. All lines are spatially extended, except , with the line emission extending up to 25 kpc from the core. emission is found to be cospatial with and CO.

With the help of FIR continuum Herschel measurements, together with a suite of coeval radio, submm and infrared data from other observatories, we performed a spectral energy distribution fitting of NGC 1275 using a model that contains contributions from dust emission as well as synchrotron AGN emission. This has allowed us to accurately estimate the dust parameters. The data indicate a low dust emissivity index, 1 , a total dust mass close to 10^7 , a cold dust component with temperature 38 2 K and a warm dust component with temperature of 116 9 K. The FIR-derived star formation rate (SFR) is 24 1 , which is in agreement with the FUV-derived SFR in the core, determined after applying corrections for both Galactic and internal reddening. The total infrared luminosity in the range 8 to 1000 is inferred to be 1.5 10^ 11 , making NGC 1275 a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1275 as the prototype gamma-ray BCG, noting its Perseus dominance, central radio source, variable high-energy emission, high gamma/radio ratio, and possible beaming.

quotes.quote: These are NGC 1275 and M 87 abdo09a,abdo09b : the dominant member galaxies of the cooling-core clusters Perseus and Virgo, respectively. Both are known to host bright central radio sources associated with the active nucleus (see vermeulen94,asada06 and biretta91,sparks96 ), and are also detected at very-high energies aleksic12,aharonian06 . The multiwavelength emission of AGN is characteristically variable, and indeed the ray flux is found to vary on timescales down to days in the case of NGC 1275 brown11 ; similarly for M 87 abramowski12 .

The ray flux-limit for the sample corresponds to only 0.1 of the flux of NGC 1275, which has F_ 1 GeV /F_ 1.4 GHz 120 ( 90 excluding ray flares). The average core radio flux is 1 of that of NGC 1275. The lack of a ray signal in the stack therefore implies that the average core-radio selected BCGs have spectral energy distributions with a different shape to that of NGC 1275, with significantly lower ray flux relative to core radio flux, though the current highly variable state of ray emission in this active galaxy kataoka10 may account for the difference in F_ 1 GeV /F_ 1.4 GHz . NGC 1275 and M 87 are the closest cooling-core BCGs, so the detected ray emission is unlikely to be beamed toward us if they are drawn from a random distribution of sight lines. However, a role for beaming in NGC 1275 cannot be excluded and may play a role in its unusual ray brightness (see above).

sources.summary: Gemini NIFS spectroscopy of NGC 1275 finds an inner H2/[Fe II] disc, a possible infalling molecular streamer, and shock-heated molecular gas near the active nucleus.

quotes.quote: We report the results of high spatial and spectral resolution integral-field spectroscopy of the central 3 3 arcsec ^2 of the active galaxy NGC 1275 (Perseus A), based on observations with the Near-infrared Integral Field Spectrograph (NIFS) and the ALTAIR adaptive-optics system on the Gemini North telescope. The circum-nuclear disc in the inner R 50 pc of NGC 1275 is seen in both the H _2 and [ Fe ii ] lines. The disc is interpreted as the outer part of a collisionally-excited turbulent accretion disc. The kinematic major axis of the disc at a position angle of 68^ is oriented perpendicular to the radio jet. A streamer-like feature to the south-west of the disc, detected in H _2 but not in [ Fe ii ] , is discussed as one of possibly several molecular streamers, presumably falling into the nuclear region.

Indications of an ionization structure within the disc are deduced from the He i and Br emission lines, which may partially originate from the inner portions of the accretion disc. The kinematics of these two lines agrees with the signature of the circum-nuclear disc, but both lines display a larger central velocity dispersion than the H _2 line. The rovibrational H _2 transitions from the core of NGC 1275 are indicative of thermal excitation caused by shocks and agree with excitation temperatures of 1360 and 4290 K for the lower- and higher-energy H _2 transitions, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1275 as the Perseus-cluster radio galaxy with an active nucleus, gas filaments, compact 3C 84 radio source, and blazar-like high-energy emission.

quotes.quote: (MAGIC collaboration) The radio galaxy NGC 1275 is the central dominant galaxy of the Perseus cluster ( Abell 426 ). It is a well-known active galactic nucleus (AGN) already included in the original study of . In fact, it is a rather complex object with very peculiar characteristics that might be the result of massive gas accretion from the cluster cooling flow or from a recent merging event con01 . The main features, in addition to the central AGN, are remarkable gas filaments that are tens of kpc long and emit strong hydrogen lines lyn70 , and a foreground galaxy that falls toward NGC 1275 cau92 . In radio, the emission is dominated by a very bright compact source ( 3C 84 ) at the center of the galaxy.

All these elements support the view that the ray emission of NGC 1275 originates from a misaligned BL Lac jet, in the same way as for the other VHE ray emitting radio galaxies, and consistent with the unification scheme (e.g., urry&padovani ). A notable exception, however, is the bulk Lorentz factor, constrained to have a lower value ( _b 2 ) than the typical _b> 10 inferred in BL Lacs. Indeed, with a jet-viewing angle _ v 30^ , a larger _b would imply a smaller Doppler factor, while our model with =2 already seems at the lower edge of the acceptable range (a lower would imply a smaller distance between the two SED peaks, inducing a too high X-ray flux).

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 84/NGC 1275 as a nearby FR I radio galaxy whose restarted VLBA jet changed from ridge-brightened to limb-brightened amid renewed radio and gamma-ray activity.

quotes.quote: We present a study of sub-pc scale radio structure of the radio galaxy 3C 84/NGC 1275 based on the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) data at 43 GHz. We discover a limb-brightening in the ``restarted" jet associated with the 2005 radio outburst. In the 1990s, the jet structure was ridge-brightening rather than limb-brightening, despite the observations being done with similar angular resolution. This indicates that the transverse jet structure has changed recently.

The radio source 3C 84 is associated with the giant elliptical/radio galaxy NGC 1275 (z=0.0176). Its classification by radio luminosity is Fanaroff-Riley type-I e.g., Chiaberge1999 . Thanks to its brightness and proximity, this source is one of the best-studied radio sources in history. Recently increased activity starting in 2005 has been detected in radio band Abdo2009 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1275/3C 84 as a variable Perseus brightest-cluster-galaxy AGN, linking radio, gamma-ray, jet, filament, and feedback behavior over days to decades.

quotes.quote: The prototypical example of AGN feedback within a cluster of galaxies can be found in the Perseus cluster bohringer93,fabian01 , host to the active brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) NGC 1275. The spectral properties of the nuclear narrow-line radio source 3C 84 place it as intermediate between a BL Lac object and a Seyfert galaxy. Significantly, NGC 1275 is one of the very few sources detected by the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope (hereafter Fermi ), that is not strongly beamed abdo09a , and exhibits pronounced flaring activity see kataoka10,brown12 . During these high-energy (HE) flares, the source becomes significantly harder brown12 , and bright enough to detect in TeV  rays aleksic12 .
Another feature observed in NGC 1275 is a spectacular optical nebulosity: a filamentary system of ionised, H emitting gas extending over 100 kpc fabian03a .

Critically, the AGN output of NGC 1275 is variable on decade-long timescales. odea84 show that NGC 1275 varied substantially over the 22 years between 1960 and 1982 at 2--100 GHz. abdo09a note that the 15 GHz flux density of NGC 1275 declined dramatically between 1982 and 2005, but has increased steadily since then.

sources.summary: The paper reports large, stable millimeter Faraday rotation toward 3C 84, linking its polarized nuclear emission to the NGC 1275 AGN, jet boundary, and subparsec disk-like accretion flow.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of Faraday rotation toward radio source 3C 84, the active galactic nucleus in NGC1275 at the core of the Perseus Cluster. The rotation measure (RM), determined from polarization observations at wavelengths of 1.3 and 0.9 mm, is ( 8.7 2.3 ) 10^5 , among the largest ever measured. The RM remained relatively constant over a 2 year period even as the intrinsic polarization position angle wrapped through a span of 300 degrees. The Faraday rotation is likely to originate either in the boundary layer of the radio jet from the nucleus, or in the accretion flow onto the central black hole. The accretion flow probably is disk-like rather than spherical on scales of less than a parsec, otherwise the RM would be even larger.

Radio source 3C 84 is associated with the active galactic nucleus (AGN) in NGC 1275, the central galaxy in the Perseus cluster, the prototypical `cooling flow' cluster Fabian1994 . The black hole in the AGN launches powerful jets into the surrounding medium. The accretion process onto the black hole has been studied through a variety of techniques on scales as small as a few parsecs Vermeulen1994, Walker1994, Walker2000, Wilman2005, Scharwachter2013 .

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 1275 as a gamma-ray radio galaxy and AGN, reporting increased MeV/GeV flux and correlated first evidence of X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 1275 is a gamma-ray-emitting radio galaxy at the center of the Perseus cluster. In the optical and X-ray region, NGC 1275 also shows a bright core, but their origin has not been understood, since a disk emission is not ruled out. In fact, NGC 1275 exhibits optical broad emission lines and a X-ray Fe-K line, which are typical for Seyfert galaxies.

In our precious studies of NGC 1275 with Suzaku/XIS, no X-ray time variability was found from 2006 to 2011, regardless of moderate gamma-ray variability observed by it Fermi LAT . We have continued monitoring observations of NGC 1275 with Suzaku/XIS. In 2013-2014, MeV/GeV gams-ray flux of NGC 1275 gradually increased and reached the maximum at the beginning of 2014. Correlated with this recent gamma-ray activity, we found that X-ray flux also increased, and this is the first evidence of X-ray variability of NGC 1275.
NGC 1275 is known as an AGN and classified as a radio-loud Seyfert galaxy or a radio galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1275 as a nearby radio galaxy with recurrent nuclear activity, a reactivated nucleus, and a new C3 radio component interpreted as an early lobe or hot spot.

quotes.quote: The nearby radio galaxy NGC 1275 (3C 84, Per A) is ideally suited to probe the physical nature of the relativistic jet in connection with its sub-parsec-scale structure observed with VLBI thanks to its proximity ( z =0.0176; 1 mas = 0.35 pc). NGC 1275 hosts many lobe-like structures on various spatial scales, indicating that it underwent recurrent nuclear activities in the past. In recent, its nucleus has been reactivated again. The Fermi Gamma-ray Spase Telescope has detected GeV ray emission much brighter than its upper limit measured by the EGRET in 1990s, which was accompanied with enhanced radio activities e.g. Abdo et al. 2009 . High-resolution observations with the VLBI Exploration of Radio Astrometry (VERA) detected an ejection of a new radio component C3 on sub-parsec scales Nagai et al. 2010 , which was already emerged in 2002 Suzuki et al. 2012 and has been accountable for recent radio activities from 2000s to 2011 e.g. Nagai et al. 2010, Suzuki et al. 2012 .

Our new observations suggest that the new radio component C3 of NGC 1275 is most likely a radio lobe and/or a hot spot at a very early stage of radio lobe evolution. This suggests that the radio lobes in radio galaxies might be already formed on sub-parsec scales at the vicinity of SMBHs as C3. For the recent active state in ray regime, our results implies that the relativistic leptons can be also supplied from the particle-acceleration region inside C3, which might play an important role to generate inverse-compton emission in ray regime.

sources.summary: The paper studies the SMBH environment in massive elliptical NGC 1275, linking the 3C 84 young radio lobe, Bondi-radius gas, jet power, and low-density accretion-axis environment.

quotes.quote: Received 0000 December 00; in original form 0000 October 00 000--000 0000 We analyse the environment of the supermassive black hole (SMBH) in the centre of a massive elliptical galaxy NGC 1275 in the Perseus cluster, hosting the radio source 3C 84. We focus on the young radio lobe observed inside the estimated Bondi accretion radius. We discuss the momentum balance between the jet associated with the lobe and the surrounding gas. The results are compared with the proper motion of the radio lobe obtained with the very long baseline interferometry. We find that under assumption of a high-density environment ( 100 cm^ 3 ), the jet power must be comparable to the Eddington luminosity --- this is clearly inconsistent with the current moderate activity of 3C 84, which indicates instead that the jet is expanding in a very low density region ( 1 cm^ 3 ), along the rotation axis of the accretion flow.

To summarize, we have studied the environment of the young radio lobe observed at 10 pc from the SMBH at the centre of the galaxy NGC 1275 (or the radio source 3C 84) in the Perseus cluster. The lobe is located inside the estimated Bondi accretion radius. The lobe should be associated with the cocoon created by jet activities in the AGN, and the evolution of the jet should depend on the jet power and the properties of the hot ambient gas.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1275 as the central dominant Perseus galaxy and AGN; MAGIC detects its point-like very-high-energy emission and measures a steep 90 GeV-1.2 TeV spectrum.

quotes.quote: The Perseus cluster also hosts three bright radio galaxies 1968MNRS138 : NGC 1275, the central dominant galaxy of the cluster, NGC 1265, archetype of a head-tail radio galaxy, in which the jets are bent by their interaction with the ICM, and IC 310, a peculiar object that shows properties of different classifications and which could be an intermediate state between a BL Lac and a radio galaxy. The AGNs of both NGC 1275 and IC 310 show a bright and variable ray emission in the energy ranges of both the Fermi Large-Area-Telescope (LAT) 2010A&A...519L...6N,2009arXiv0904.1904T and the atmospheric-Cherenkov telescopes 2010ApJ...723L.207A,2012A&A...539L...2A, 2014A&A...564A...5A,2014A&A...563A..91A . The NGC 1275 AGN emission obscures the expected diffuse cluster emission over most of the ray band, in particular in the GeV region, where the spectral energy distribution is peaking 2014A&A...564A...5A .

Above 250 GeV, the emission from NGC 1275 is detected with a significance of 8.5 and a signal-to-background ratio greater than 20 . Above 1 TeV, however, no source other than IC 310 is detected. Figure fig:NGC1275_theta_plot compares the excess ( ray) event distribution above 250 GeV as a function of the squared distance from NGC 1275 ( ^2 ) with the MAGIC point spread function (PSF) obtained from contemporaneous Crab nebula data at similar zenith angles. In this ^2 plot, the PSF is described by a double exponential (corresponding to a double Gaussian in a 2D map), which matches the excess shape of the Crab nebula The Crab nebula has a much harder spectrum than NGC 1275, so the PSF for NGC 1275 could be slightly larger. The PSF shown in Figure fig:NGC1275_theta_plot has been normalised to fit the NGC 1275 signal.
The emission from the NGC 1275 AGN, however, overlies the searched signal region. We derived the most precise NGC 1275 spectrum in the range 90 GeV--1.2 TeV ever done.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1275 central Seyfert AGN as an unusually bright Chandra target with over half a million X-ray counts for ALP spectral-irregularity searches.

quotes.quote: Ultra-deep Chandra observations of the Perseus cluster contain over 5 10^5 counts from the AGN of the central cluster galaxy NGC1275, and represent a dataset of extraordinary quality for ALP searches. We use this dataset to search for X-ray spectral irregularities from the AGN. The absence of irregularities at the O (30 ) level allows us to place leading constraints on the ALP-photon mixing parameter g_ a 1.4 - 4.0 10^ 12 GeV ^ 1 for m_a 10^ 12 eV , depending on assumptions on the magnetic field realisation along the line of sight. Axion-like particles, NGC1275 The Perseus galaxy cluster (A426) is the brightest X-ray cluster in the sky. It is a cool-core cluster at redshift z=0.0176 , centered around the Seyfert galaxy NGC1275 and its Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN). Due to its proximity and brightness, the Perseus cluster has been a standard target for all X-ray satellites.

In contrast, the AGN at the centre of NGC1275 is both exceedingly bright and the subject of enormous observational time. NGC1275 has been observed for 1 Ms by Chandra ACIS-S, with observations taken in 2002 and 2004, and also for a further 0.5 Ms by Chandra ACIS-I in 2009. Taken together, these generate over half a million X-ray counts from the central AGN -- a dataset of extraordinary quality for searching for spectral irregularities.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1275 brightened in X-rays with GeV gamma rays, shows rapid Swift flares, and discusses jet synchrotron and shock-in-jet interpretations.

quotes.quote: We analyzed Suzaku /XIS data of 2006--2015 observations of a gamma-ray emitting radio galaxy NGC 1275, and brightening of the nucleus in the X-ray band was found in 2013--2015, correlating with GeV Gamma-ray brightening. This is the first evidence of variability with correlation between GeV gamma-ray and X-ray for NGC 1275. We also analyzed Swift /XRT data of NGC 1275, and found that X-ray was flaring by a factor of 5 in several days in 2006, 2011, and 2013. The X-ray spectrum during the flare was featureless and somewhat steeper with a photon index of 2 against 1.7 in the normal state, indicating that a synchrotron component became brighter. A large Xray to GeV gamma-ray flux ratio in the flare could be explained by the shock-in-jet scenario.

NGC 1275 is an elliptical galaxy, locating at the center of the Perseus cluster, and is also known to host an AGN and thus classified as a radio-loud Seyfert galaxy or a radio galaxy. Radio galaxies are very important to study jet phenomena. A viewing angle of their jets is not so small as blazars, and thus the apparent enhancement of the jet core emission due to the beaming effect is not so significant and we can probe the jet structure by seeing the jet periphery emission by observing radio galaxies. In recent years, Fermi gamma-ray space telescope detected GeV gamma-ray emission from radio galaxies Abdo10 , and NGC 1275 is the brightest among them. Since NGC 1275 was not detected with CGRO /EGRET and the gamma-ray flux of Fermi is much higher than the EGRET sensitivity limit, gamma-ray emission from NGC 1275 turned out to have increased over ten times Abdo09 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1275 as the central Perseus giant with gas and dust filaments, old halo GCs plus younger clusters, and a rough GC population estimate near 13,500.

quotes.quote: NGC 1272 ( M_V^T = -22.96 ) and NGC 1275 ( M_V^T = -22.61 ) are the two dominant member galaxies of Perseus and have similar visual luminosities. In this respect Perseus is analogous to several other rich clusters where two major E galaxies of roughly equal luminosity dominate (for example, NGC 4472 and 4486 in Virgo, or NGC 4874 and 4889 in Coma). However, NGC 1275 appears to be the closest to the dynamical center of Perseus, based on its projected location virtually at the center of the hot X-ray halo, its radial velocity (5264 km s ^ 1 ) close to the cluster mean, and the dramatic presence of large gas inflow revealed in its extensive gas and dust network of filaments.

The star-cluster population in NGC 1275 itself has been discussed briefly by carlson_etal1998 , canning_etal2010 , and , derived from WFPC2 and ACS images centered on the galaxy (though in different filters) that we do not use here. In total, the NGC 1275 star clusters consist of a mixture of old GCs spread throughout the galaxy's halo, plus a rich population of younger clusters located along its spectacular network of H filaments. Since NGC 1275 is the central giant, we first plot the GC number density versus radial distance from it. The results are shown in Figure fig:radial . Here, the number of GCs per unit projected area on the sky _ cl is calculated within radial annuli centered on NGC 1275.

Extrapolating over all magnitudes as described above gives N_ GC = 13500 2000 and a specific frequency S_N = 12.2 1.8 , including 0.1- mag uncertainties in the GCLF turnover and dispersion as above. This total GC population and S_N would make NGC 1275 quite similar to M87 in Virgo e.g. harris2009b .

sources.summary: The paper presents specialized Chandra imaging of NGC 1275, linking its black hole, Fe K emission, low X-ray continuum, radio flaring, and feedback in Perseus.

quotes.quote: Recent observations with Hitomi have resolved the narrow Fe K line associated with accretion onto the black hole in NGC 1275 (3C 84), the active galaxy at the center of Perseus. Here, we report on a specialized Chandra imaging observation of NGC 1275 that offers a complementary angle. A low X-ray continuum flux was likely measured from NGC 1275; contemporaneously, radio flaring and record-high GeV fluxes were recorded.

NGC 1275 is the galaxy at the heart of the Perseus Cluster ( z=0.017284 , Hitomi Collaboration 2017); accordingly, it harbors a massive very black hole ( M_ BH = 3.4 10^ 8 M_ , Wilman et al. \ 2005; M_ BH = 8 10^ 8 M_ , Scharwachter et al. \ 2013). Currently, at least, the black hole in NGC 1275 radiates at a low fraction of its Eddington limit ( L/L_ Edd 3 10^ 4 , Sikora et al. \ 2007), below the quasar and Seyfert regimes. The Fe K line in NGC 1275 has been studied in several prior observations, notably by XMM-Newton (Churazov et al. \ 2003).

We have therefore analyzed a special Chandra observation of the massive black hole in NGC 1275 (3C 84), which drives strong feedback into the Perseus cluster. Our analysis of the 6.0--6.4 keV image of NGC 1275 suggests that the Fe K emission line region is likely smaller than about 98 pc in size, consistent with an extended molecular torus or disk in NGC 1275 (Wilman et al. \ 2005, Sharwachter et al. \ 2013), representing the outermost accretion flow.

sources.summary: The paper follows up evidence for a 3.5 keV absorption dip in NGC1275, the central Perseus AGN, and revisits both recent and archival Chandra spectra.

quotes.quote: More recently, evidence has also appeared for the line in absorption, in particular in the spectrum of NGC1275, the central AGN of the Perseus cluster. In , analysis of highly off-axis Chandra observations of NGC1275 found evidence at around 4 - 5 for a dip at 3.54 0.02 keV. The significance is quoted approximately. The precise value of the significance, measured by the ^2 improvement when a negative Gaussian is added to the fit, can always be gamed by modifying either the energy range of the spectral fit or the spatial extraction region used for the AGN. The best-fit strength for this dip corresponds to an equivalent width of 15 eV (which compares to 1 eV for the line in emission).

We also revisit the analysis of the NGC1275 spectrum from the older on-axis ACIS-S observations of the Perseus core that were taken in 2002 and 2004. These were taken on a standard frame time of 3.2s, but at a time when the AGN was much less bright than in 2016 - leading to a poorer contrast against the cluster emission despite a much deeper exposure than for the 2016 data. As for the 2016 data, the center of the image is highly piled and it is necessary to exclude this region.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1275/3C 84 as the Perseus central FR I/Seyfert galaxy and reports MAGIC VHE gamma-ray flares with extreme flux, rapid variability, and hard curved spectra.

quotes.quote: NGC 1275, also known as 3C 84, is the central galaxy of the Perseus cluster with a redshift of z=0.0176 falco1999 . While the optical spectrum shows strong nuclear emission lines typical of a Seyfert galaxy Humason1932, Khachikian1974 , the morphology in the radio band reveals a Fanaroff-Riley I type Vermeulen1994, Buttiglione2010 . The viewing angle of NCG 1275 was inferred from radio interferometers to be =30^ 55^ by walker1994 and =65^ 16^ Fujita2017 on (sub)-parsec scales.

We report on the detection of flaring activity from the Fanaroff-Riley I radio galaxy NGC 1275 in very-high-energy (VHE, E > 100 GeV) gamma rays with the MAGIC telescopes. The observations were performed between 2016 September and 2017 February, as part of a monitoring program. The brightest outburst with 1.5 times the Crab Nebula flux above 100 GeV (C.U.) was observed during the night between 2016 December 31 and 2017 January 1. The flux is fifty times higher than the mean flux previously measured in two observational campaigns between 2009 October and 2010 February and between 2010 August and 2011 February. Significant variability of the day-by-day light curve was measured.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 84/NGC 1275 as an active galaxy laboratory where KaVA monitoring found a parsec-scale jet-head flip consistent with interaction with clumpy circumnuclear gas.

quotes.quote: By performing the multi-epoch monitoring observation with the KVN and VERA Array (KaVA) at 43 GHz, we investigate the kinematics of the notable newborn bright component C3 located at the tip of the recurrent jet of 3C 84. During 2015 August-September, we discover the flip of C3 and the amount of the flip is about 0.4 milli-arcsecond in angular scale, which corresponds to 0.14 parsec in physical scale. After the flip of C3, it wobbled at the same location for a few months and then it restarted to propagate towards the southern direction. The flux density of C3 coherently showed the monotonic increase during the observation period.

NGC 1275 is a notable nearby giant elliptical galaxy at the core of the Perseus cluster, with an optically luminous nucleus, currently classified as a Seyfert 1.5/LINER (Sosa-Brito et al. 2001). Interestingly, Nagai et al. (2010) found the emergence of newborn bright component so-called C3 during multi-epoch monitoring of 3C 84 using Very Long Baseline Interferometer (VLBI). The component C3 showed a proper motion towards the southern direction with the apparent velocity of 0.2-0.3 (Nagai et al. 2010; Suzuki et al. 2012; Hiura et al. 2018). Since various observations of 3C 84 indicate the gas-rich environment such as molecular gas (Krabbe et al. 2000; Salome et a. 2006; Lim et al 2008), warm [ H _ 2 ] gas (Wilman et al. 2005; Scharwachter et al. 2013) and dense ionized plasma surrounding the 3C 84 jet (O'Dea 1984; Walker et al. 2000), 3C 84 is regarded as a quite unique laboratory to explore interactions between the jet and the circumnuclear environment on parsec scale.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC1275 as a radio galaxy and possible cosmic-ray/neutrino source, where reconnection-accelerated CRs can reproduce the VHE tail and PeV neutrino fluxes below IceCube limits.

quotes.quote: Very high energy (VHE) emission has been detected from the radio galaxy NGC1275, establishing it as a potential cosmic-ray (CR) accelerator and a high energy neutrino source. We here study neutrino and ray emission from the core of NGC1275 simulating the interactions of CRs assumed to be accelerated by magnetic reconnection, with the accreting plasma environment. To do this, we combine (i) numerical general relativistic (GR) magneto-hydrodynamics (MHD), (ii) Monte Carlo GR leptonic radiative transfer and, (iii) Monte Carlo interaction of CRs. A leptonic emission model that reproduces the SED in the [ 10^3 10^ 10.5 ] eV energy range is used as the background target for photo-pion interactions+electromagnetic cascading. CRs injected with the power-law index κ=1.3 produce an emission profile that matches the VHE tail of NGC1275.

The very high energy emission tail of NGC1275 is better reproduced with the hadronic models with smaller power-law index of CR injection, (which are compatible with the predictions of reconnection acceleration). The associated neutrino fluxes are consistent with the upper limits from IceCube (see Fig. fig2 , right panel) and from the models presented here, we conclude that the core of NGC1275 would mostly contribute to neutrino detections at PeV energies. However, the neutrino fluxes presented in Fig. fig2 may be over-estimations, since they come from a single source.

sources.summary: The paper images 3C 84/NGC 1275 with mm-VLBI, finding compact core polarization, high rotation measure, and evidence that Faraday rotation arises in a stratified jet boundary layer.

quotes.quote: We report results from a deep polarization imaging of the nearby radio galaxy 3C 84 (NGC 1275). The source was observed with the Global Millimeter VLBI Array (GMVA) at 86 GHz at an ultra-high angular resolution of 50 as (corresponding to 250 R_ s ). We also add complementary multi-wavelength data from the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA; 15 43 GHz) and from the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA; 97.5, 233.0, and 343.5 GHz). At 86 GHz, we measure a fractional linear polarization of 2 in the VLBI core region. The polarization morphology suggests that the emission is associated with an underlying limb-brightened jet.

The radio galaxy 3C 84 (Perseus A, NGC 1275) is an intriguing source whose linear polarization in the core region at cm-wavelengths is extremely weak ( <0.1 at 15 GHz; taylor06 ). This is much lower than typically observed in other AGN jets lister05 . 3C 84 is also a relatively nearby source at a redshift of z=0.0176 and a luminosity distance of d_ L =75  Mpc 3c84_dist We assume CDM cosmology with H_ 0 =71 km/s/Mpc, _ =0.73 and _ M =0.27 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1275 as a nearby radio/brightest cluster galaxy with complex cold molecular gas, Seyfert/LINER nuclear emission, and filamentary gas linked to feeding and feedback.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations of the CO(2-1), HCN(3-2), and HCO ^ + (3-2) lines in the nearby radio galaxy / brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of NGC 1275 with the spatial resolution of 20 pc. In the previous observations, the CO(2-1) emission was detected as radial filaments lying in the east-west direction on kpc scale. We resolved the inner filament and found that the filament cannot be represented by a simple infalling stream on sub-kpc scale. The observed complex nature of the filament resembles the cold gas structure predicted by numerical simulations of cold chaotic accretion.

The cluster harbors the radio galaxy / brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) NGC 1275, centered at the Perseus cluster. Nuclear emission of NGC 1275 was originally classified as a Seyfert 2 Seyfert1943 but later has been also classified as a Seyfert 1.5/LINER Veron2006, Sosa-Brito2001 because of the presence of broad H wing.

In contrast to other nearby BCGs such as M87 Tan2008, Perlman2007 , NGC 1275 is the reservoir of a large amount of cold gas ( M_ gas 10^ 10 M_ ) with filamentary structures on kpc scales Salome2006, Salome2008, Lim2008 , which is reminiscent of a cooling flow. These properties make NGC 1275 the ideal target to study the feeding and feedback between the central super massive black hole (SMBH) and the surrounding environment.

sources.summary: The paper revisits ALP constraints from NGC 1275, modeling regular magnetic fields in its Perseus-cluster X-ray cavity and showing weaker spectral-irregularity constraints than turbulent-field assumptions.

quotes.quote: NGC 1275 is a radio galaxy located in the center of the Perseus cluster. It is a bright X-ray and gamma-ray source. Thanks to the intensity of the source, sufficient statistics could be collected to obtain detailed spectra suitable for the search for spectral features at high confidence level, which justified the choice of this source as a target for several attempts to constrain ALP parameters from the lack of irregularities in the spectrum.

In the center of the Perseus cluster, like in many other clusters containing a large active galaxy, an X-ray cavity was observed, spatially coinciding with the so-called radio mini-halo  . This cavity most probably is a result of the interaction between outflows of NGC 1275 and the intracluster gas.

Here, we revisit ALP constraints from gamma-ray observations of NGC 1275 taking into account the regular magnetic field of the X-ray cavity observed around this radio galaxy. This field was not accounted for in previous studies, which assumed a model of purely turbulent fields with coherence length much smaller than the cavity size. For the purely regular field, ALP constraints are relaxed considerably, compared to the purely turbulent one.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1275 as a nearby FR I/Seyfert-like radio galaxy and studies multiwavelength variability, explaining activity-state changes with jet emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1275 is one of the brightest nearby radio galaxies (z =0.0176 ; falco1999 ) situated at the centre of the Perseus cluster. The optical spectra of this galaxy exhibit strong emission lines which are a typical feature of Seyfert galaxies humason1932,khachikian1974 . However, in the radio band, this source is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley type I (FR I type) radio galaxy with a compact central source and an extended jet e.g., Vermeulen1994,Asada2006,buttiglione2010 .

We carried out a detailed study of the temporal and broadband spectral behaviour of one of the brightest misaligned active galaxies in rays, NGC 1275 utilising 11 years of Fermi , and available Swift and AstroSat observations. Based on the cumulative flux distribution of the ray lightcurve, we identified four distinct activity states and noticed an increase in the baseline flux during the first three states. A large flaring activity in rays was noticed in the fourth state. The increase in the average baseline flux from state 1 to state 3 can be explained considering a corresponding increase of jet particle normalisation. The inverse Comptonisation of synchrotron photons explained the average X-ray and ray emission by jet electrons during the first three longer duration states.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 84 as the bright radio core of NGC 1275, linking its radio jet kinematics and GeV-TeV emission to gamma-ray flaring activity.

quotes.quote: Box 800, 9700 AV Groningen, The Netherlands Max-Planck-Institut f u Radioastronomie, Auf dem H u gel 69, 53121, Bonn, Germany 3C 84 (NGC 1275) is the bright radio core of the Perseus Cluster. Even in the absence of strong relativistic effects, the source has been detected at rays up to TeV energies. Despite its intensive study, the physical processes responsible for the high-energy emission in the source remain unanswered. We present a detailed kinematics study of the source and its connection to ray emission.

We present here the jet kinematics study of a nearby misaligned radio galaxy 3C 84 z=0.017559, 3c84_z and its connection with the ray flaring activity. The source is the radio counterpart of the Seyfert Type 1.5 galaxy NGC 1275 (also known as Perseus A) ho97 . The Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) has been detecting GeV emission from the source since the beginning of its operation in 2008 fermi2009 . TeV_detection confirmed the detection of TeV emission ( > 100 GeV) from the source, followed by several extremely bright and exceptionally rapid TeV flares detected in 2016-2017 3c84_atel16_1, 3c84_atel16_2, 3c84_atel16_3, 3c84_atel16_4, 3c84_atel16_5 .

sources.summary: NGC 1275 is a radio/giant elliptical galaxy with cold gas and a rotating CND; the paper detects diffuse synchrotron aligned with its molecular disk, linking it to supernova-driven star formation.

quotes.quote: We report the very-long-baseline-interferometry (VLBI) detection of the diffuse synchrotron emission on a scale of several tens-pc coinciding spatially with the molecular gas disk recently discovered by ALMA observations in NGC 1275. The synchrotron emissions are most likely resulted from the relativistic electrons produced by the supernova explosions. This is an unambiguous evidence of the star formation activity in a CND.

NGC 1275 (z=0.01755) is a nearby radio galaxy/giant elliptical galaxy at the center of the Perseus cluster. The radio source of NGC 1275 is also known as 3C 84 and shows multiple radio lobes on different angular scales Silver1998, Walker2000, Nagai2010 . This galaxy is a reservoir of a large amount of cold gas M_ gas 10^ 9 M_ : Lim2008, Salome2008 . On kpc scales, molecular gas filaments are aligned in the east-west direction Lim2008 and these filaments seem to coincide with the H nebulae Fabian2008 . Within 100 pc, recent ALMA observations detected a rotating CND with the CO(2-1), HCN(3-2), and HCO ^ + (3-2) lines Nagai2019 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 84/NGC 1275 as a nearby Seyfert radio galaxy, using VLBI core-shift measurements to place the jet apex upstream and infer mixed magnetic-field topology.
